Givat Amos 3-room, ₪3,500 — the cluster's best kitchen (oven + gas + dishwasher), bay view, explicitly couple-friendly PURSUE
Nesher · גבעת עמוס · ₪3,500/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,425/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪275 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is the best in the cluster: white cabinets with a freestanding gas range (oven + 4-burner cooktop), a dishwasher, fridge, a long run of prep counter and a dining table — a genuine cook's kitchen for two. The rest matches: a bright, fully furnished 3-room flat with three sofas and a sweeping Haifa-bay view, two bathrooms (tub + separate toilet), and a bedroom with wardrobe + desk. It is a 5-minute walk to the Technion (Nesher gate) with a bus stop to Haifa opposite the door and a minimarket nearby, the ad explicitly says it suits a couple and allows a longer stay, and entry is 1 Oct 2026 — within the Sep–Nov window. Several photos (staged rooms, a cat, other balconies) are scraper contamination; the genuine unit is the terrazzo-floored one in photos 00–07.
+
  • Excellent kitchen: oven + 4-burner gas + dishwasher + fridge + long counter + dining table
  • Fully furnished ('קומפלט') and move-in ready
  • Bright, spacious living room with a wide Haifa-bay view — room for two work desks
  • Explicitly 'suitable for a couple'; 5-min walk to the Technion, bus to Haifa at the door, minimarket nearby
  • Entry 1 Oct 2026 fits the Sep–Nov window; two bathrooms (tub + separate toilet)
–
  • Priciest of the strong options at ₪3,500 (still under the ₪4,500 budget)
  • Arnona ₪550/2mo; no parking; no balcony
  • Photo set is partly contaminated with other apartments — verify the real unit
  • Listing is framed annual; short-term not explicitly stated
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month furnished let (the ad leans long-term but mentions flexibility)
  • Confirm which photos are this exact unit and that the kitchen shown (oven + dishwasher) is included
  • Ask about quiet on Havatzelet St and whether 1 Oct can flex earlier into September

The kitchen is the standout: a full-size freestanding gas range (four burners + oven), a generous grey countertop, full cabinetry, a full-size fridge and a microwave, plus a separate dining table — genuinely good for a couple cooking most meals. At 62 m² it is large and bright for the price, furnished with a bed, wardrobe and balcony, and the listing explicitly offers flexibility on all rental terms (flagged short-term). The bathroom is older but clean; it is a 1-room open layout on the top (3rd) floor.

Masada St sublet, ₪3,000 all bills included — homey, work-ready, Carmelit at the door; best fit in Haifa PURSUE
Haifa · מסדה · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,000 + arnona n/a · מרוהט וכלל את כל החשבונות... מרוהט כולל הכול · missing arnona not stated
A genuinely lived-in, warm 2.5-room (60sqm) sublet on Masada — Haifa's bohemian cafe street in Hadar. Photos show a real home: plant-filled living room with big sofa and book shelves, double bed, decorated bathroom with tub, a kitchen with a long counter run and full-size fridge, and a bright enclosed sun-balcony that makes a natural second workspace. Ad explicitly offers a sublet with ALL bills included, a work corner and fast fiber. Carmelit Masada station is on the street (ride down to Paris Sq, ~10 min walk to Merkaz HaShmona train for Tel Aviv), 24/7 supermarket across the road, Talpiot produce market ~8 min walk. The main risks are calendar fit (posted for 22.7 entry) and that no photo shows the cooktop/oven.
+
  • Sublet — short-term is the listing's whole premise; no landlord-agreement gamble
  • ₪3,000 with all bills included → ~₪1,500/mo under budget
  • Fully furnished and genuinely inviting: plants, real sofa, equipped kitchen with generous counter, bathtub
  • Two workable work-spots (stated work corner + enclosed sun balcony) and fast fiber internet — ideal for two remote workers
  • Carmelit station on the street, 24/7 super opposite, cafes downstairs, Talpiot market walkable — car-free living
  • Kibbutz Hazorea ~30–35 min drive / direct-ish bus via Yokneam — easy family visits
–
  • Availability posted from 22.7 — the sublet window may not stretch into a Sept–Nov 2-month stay
  • Cooktop/oven not visible in any photo (ad says 'equipped kitchen' only)
  • Masada is a nightlife/cafe street — evening noise likely, and it's floor 1
  • Vintage building — charming but expect old-building quirks (no elevator, older windows)
?
  • FIRST question: does the sublet cover ~2 months inside Sept–Nov 2026? (posted entry 22.7)
  • Verify stove + oven exist and work (they cook most meals, vegan/GF)
  • Ask whether bedroom faces the street or the back — bar noise on weekends
  • Confirm 'all bills' covers arnona + electricity + internet explicitly

KITCHEN is the standout: a full run of taupe cabinets with a long white countertop, a proper 4-burner gas range WITH a built-in oven, a large fridge, and a stacked washer/dryer — easily the best cooking setup in this cluster for a couple who cooks most meals. The rest of the unit matches: modern grey-porcelain floors, a wood-slat feature wall, a big L-shaped sofa, and a fully renovated grey-marble bathroom with a glass rain shower. At 60m² it is also the largest and most move-in-ready of the five, though it sits at the top of the budget.
+
  • Excellent full kitchen: 4-burner gas stovetop AND oven, generous counter/prep space, large fridge, plus washer+dryer
  • Fully renovated and tastefully furnished (sectional sofa, TV, feature wall) — truly move-in ready
  • Largest unit at 60m², room for two to work from laptops
  • Flexible entry date (כניסה: גמיש) — encouraging for a short-term arrangement
  • 5-min walk to the market = walkable fresh produce (good for vegan/GF cooking); train to Tel Aviv ~25 min
–
  • ₪4,500 — top of budget, and arnona (~₪200/2mo) is NOT included, so effective cost is a bit higher
  • Ramla is the least quiet of the cluster (near the market) and a bit further from the Gedera family than Rehovot/Kiryat Ekron
  • No dedicated desk shown; two remote workers would share the living-room surfaces
  • Currently occupied/lived-in — availability timing needs checking
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) let is acceptable — listing is framed as annual; the flexible entry date is a good sign, verify the seller will do short-term
  • Ask whether the oven is gas or electric and confirm the range works
  • Ask about street noise/quiet given proximity to the market, and about parking if they end up with a car
  • Confirm arnona amount and what 'some items stay' means for the furniture

The kitchen is a genuine, usable galley: white cabinets top and bottom, a black granite counter, sink, microwave and a freestanding gas stove with oven — real prep space plus both oven AND cooktop, the couple's top need. At ₪2,250 it is by far the cheapest option, a spacious 3.5-room / 85 m² apartment that is actually lived-in and furnished (sofas, beds, TV, dining table, and a desk for laptop work), in a neighborhood the owner describes as quiet and pleasant and 'really close to the Metronit.' It is the only listing here offering an explicit short lease (6 months), a strong plus for a short-term couple. Note the listing pads in several staged/stock photos that do not match the real unit, so the actual condition should be verified.
+
  • Cheapest by far (₪2,250, well under the ₪4,500 budget)
  • Real kitchen with oven + gas cooktop + black granite counter + ample cabinets (the #1 priority)
  • 3.5 rooms / 85 m² — spacious for two remote workers; a desk/workspace is visible in photos
  • Explicit 6-month lease — the only short-term-friendly term in the cluster
  • Quiet neighborhood (Sderot Yoseftal), owner notes it is 'really close to the Metronit'
  • Already furnished and lived-in: sofas, beds, TV, dining table, closet, fridge
–
  • Described as only 'partially furnished' — owner may remove some items before handover
  • 6-month contract is longer than their ~2-month need; may require negotiating a shorter term or overpaying
  • Listing mixes in staged/stock photos also seen in the other Kiryat Ata listings, lowering photo confidence
  • Floor 3 of 4 — elevator presence uncertain in an older block
  • Bathroom is tight (washing machine crammed beside the shower stall)
  • Kiryat Ata has no train; Tel Aviv trips are ~1.5h+ by Metronit+train (minor, since both work remotely)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month stay is possible, or whether they can sub-let within the 6-month term
  • Confirm exactly which furniture stays vs is removed
  • Verify the gas cooktop and oven work and are included
  • Ask about elevator access and street/neighbor noise
  • Check car-free transit/route to Kibbutz Hazorea (~35-50 min by car; longer without one)

Bright, fully-equipped 3-month Lod sublet near Ganei Aviv train — huge budget headroom, but cooking is on portables PURSUE
Lod · גני אביב · ₪2,750/mo rent · 2 rooms · 52 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,560/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,750 + arnona ₪160 + est. utilities ₪650
Explicit 3-month sublet (from Aug 7) in Ganei Aviv, Lod's self-contained northern neighborhood — dates fit Sept-Oct perfectly. Photos show a clean, light-filled 2-room flat on floor 11 (2 elevators, floor mamad): sofa+TV+dining table, a desk in the second room, and a kitchen with real counter runs and full cabinetry — but cooking is a portable 2-burner plus a toaster-oven on the fridge, no built-in stove/oven. All-in ≈ ₪3,300 (2,750 + 160 arnona stated + bills), leaving ~₪1,200/mo headroom. Ganei Aviv rail station is ~1.3km (18-min walk or short bus), ~20-25 min to TLV; Carrefour Market, Holmes Place and clinics ~5 min walk.
+
  • True sublet, 3 months from Aug 7 — exactly their window, no landlord-agreement gamble
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,300 — well under budget; headroom funds co-working or a car
  • Real kitchen counters + full cabinets; fridge, washer, kettle present ('arrive with clothes only')
  • Bright living room; clean renovated condition; floor 11 with 2 elevators and floor-level mamad
  • Workspace: desk in second room + dining table = two laptop spots
  • Ganei Aviv train station ~18-min walk (or bus), ~20-25 min to Tel Aviv; Lod hub nearby for Jerusalem/south lines
  • Carrefour Market, kupot holim, country club within ~5 min walk; quiet residential-towers street
  • Reasonable drive (~25-30 min) to Gedera-side family
–
  • No built-in stovetop or oven — cooking on portable burners + toaster oven (counters are good, so workable, but a real minus for daily vegan+GF cooking)
  • Washer lives in the kitchen, eating some counter length
  • No bedroom/bed photo among the 5 pictures — bed unverified
  • Ganei Aviv is pleasant but isolated from Lod center; Lod outside the enclave is rough around the edges
?
  • Confirm exact sublet window and whether a ~2-month (Sept-Oct) stay within the 3 months is acceptable
  • Ask for a bedroom photo / confirm double bed and mattress condition
  • Confirm cooking setup: gas/electric portable burners included? OK to add an oven/burner they buy?
  • Ask typical monthly electricity+water+vaad to firm up the ~₪3,300 all-in estimate
  • Verify train frequency from Lod Ganei Aviv station at commute hours (some services skip it; Lod main station is the fallback, 4km bus ride)

Kitchen is the standout: an L-shaped fitted kitchen with a gas cooktop, a built-in oven, a dishwasher, a large side-by-side fridge, and a breakfast-bar island that adds genuine second-surface prep space — confirmed from photos, not just the ad text. It's a renovated, bright 80 m2 / 4-room flat with 3-way airflow, two full bathrooms, and an existing desk workspace in the bedroom, so both partners can work remotely without fighting for space. The catch is price (₪4,500 is the ceiling) and that the furniture belongs to the outgoing tenant and is offered only for a 'symbolic fee', so furnished status must be nailed down.

  • Best kitchen by far: gas cooktop + oven + dishwasher + big fridge + island/breakfast-bar counter — ideal for a couple cooking most meals

Warm, arty private house on Bialik St in Kadima-Tzoran, subletting Aug 15 - Oct 14 (possibly earlier entry). Photos show a genuine cook's kitchen (5-burner gas hob in long stone counters, Delft-tile backsplash, island + full dining), a double-height living room with fireplace and an antique writing desk, bright bedrooms, and a landscaped 'magical' garden with patio and pergola. Sublet-board price ₪1,200/mo + stated arnona ₪750 → all-in ≈ ₪2,700-2,900 even with big-house bills — deep under budget IF the price is real (symbolic house-minding rents do happen; a dog bowl is visible, so duties are likely). Weakness: transport — no train in town; buses 55/56 to Beit Yehoshua station (~25 min) then ~20 min train → ~1h15 to TLV; car recommended.
+
  • Real, generous kitchen: 5-burner gas hob, long counters, island — built for people who cook
  • Inviting character home: double-height living space, fireplace, antique desk (ready workspace), bright bedrooms
  • Stated cost is a bargain: ₪1,200 + ₪750 arnona + bills ≈ ₪2,700-2,900 all-in — ~₪1,600/mo headroom (funds a rental car)
  • Big landscaped garden with shaded seating — great for heat-season living
  • Explicit sublet with dates; quiet suburban street, 5-min walk to Ilanot forest
  • 6 rooms — two separate work rooms trivially
–
  • Sublet window ends Oct 14 and requires ~Aug 15 start — their 2 months would be Aug 15-Oct 14, only partly inside Sept-Nov (OK only with their stated flexibility)
  • Transport is the weak leg: bus-to-train commute ~1h15 to TLV; car strongly recommended for Roy's weekly trips
  • ₪1,200/mo is suspiciously low — likely house-minding duties (dog bowl visible: possible pet care), or a data/typo risk
  • Stated arnona ₪750/mo is high (big house) and big-house electricity in Sept heat adds up
  • Lived-in family home: they'd live among the owners' belongings
  • Far from Gedera (~50 min) and Hazorea (~45 min)
?
  • VERIFY the ₪1,200/mo price is real and monthly, and exactly what it covers (arnona ₪750 — who pays? bills?)
  • Ask about duties: dog/cat care, garden watering, pool upkeep — what's expected
  • Confirm entry/exit flexibility: can they start earlier/later, and is Aug 15-Oct 14 acceptable vs their Sept-Nov preference
  • Confirm there's a working oven (hob clearly seen; oven not in frame; ad says 'all appliances')
  • Check bus 55/56 first/last times vs Roy's TLV days; price a 2-month rental car from the savings
  • Confirm internet quality (remote work) — suburban house, likely fine but ask
75
Koresh St Afula: designer-grade renovation, huge counter, ₪2,500 stated ALL-IN — best value-for-condition in the cluster; no oven PURSUE
Afula · ₪2,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,000 + arnona ₪200 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · 2000 שכירות+ 150 חשמל+ 200 ארנונה+ 100 מים+ 50 ועד בית=2500 כולל חשבונות
confirm short-term
Agency (Nadlan Afula Yazamut) 2-room, 50sqm on the 4th floor, renovated to a genuinely high standard: long black kitchen with marble-look worktop giving the most prep space of any listing here, huge corner sofa, double bed + wardrobe + mirror + TV, luxury bathroom. The ad itemizes the full cost: 2,000 + 150 electricity + 200 arnona + 100 water + 50 va'ad = ₪2,500 including bills. East-central location is also the closest of the Afula listings to the train station (~10-15 min walk). Cooking is hob + microwave — no oven listed; with this much counter, a bought portable oven solves it.
+
  • Stated ₪2,500/mo ALL-IN — ₪2,000 headroom; cheapest fully-priced renovated option
  • Exceptional condition: brand-new renovation, modern bathroom, 'huge' rooms for 2-rm layout
  • Kitchen counter is generous — best prep surface in the cluster; fridge, microwave, hob, washing machine, 2 ACs
  • Closest Afula listing to the rail station (~1km): hourly Valley-line trains, plus central bus station and direct 825 bus to TLV
  • Bright: white walls, large windows, spotless floors; pets allowed
–
  • No oven stated or visible (only כיריים) — they'd buy a portable/toaster oven
  • No dining table or desk in photos/inventory — their own desk covers one workstation, second needs improvising
  • 4th floor, no elevator mentioned
  • East-of-center address (~15-20 min walk to the center's shops), quieter but less walkable to the shuk
  • Agency ad — commission question on a 2-month stay
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Verify what furniture is actually included beyond photos (dining table? desk allowed?)
  • Ask if landlord adds an oven or agrees to them installing a portable one
  • Confirm agency fee treatment for short lets
  • Verify exact address/floor and elevator

Dated but fully-equipped 2.5-room on HaHagana - real oven + gas hob at 2,400 PURSUE
Kiryat Bialik · ₪2,400/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,300/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,400 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is the real draw here: a freestanding gas range (oven plus gas cooktop), a microwave and a tall fridge in dated blue cabinetry - the only clearly-shown unit with both an oven and a hob, exactly the cooking capability this couple needs, though the counter is a single modest run rather than a big prep surface. The apartment is fully furnished (beds, wardrobes, sofa, dining table, washer, two AC units) and described as renovated on a quiet street, but it looks dated - blue kitchen, old terrazzo floors, patterned couch. At 40 m2 / 2.5 rooms and 2,400 it is the best-value genuine cook's kitchen in this cluster.

  • Quiet street, renovated per listing, shelter 80 m away
  • Kiryat Bialik - Metronit + train access

  • Kitchen counter / prep space is modest (single run)

  • Photos 06-17 are 'similar listings' contamination; only 00-05 are this unit

Herzliya · Neve Amal · ?/mo rent · facebook · added 2026-07-15
no photos
Furnished housing unit in Neve Amal, a quiet 'kibbutz-vibe' Herzliya neighborhood, offered exactly 6.9–5.11 (the couple's 2-month window) while the owners travel. Photos show a desk + office chair, so there's a real workspace for two remote workers, and it's 15 min from Gaash beach/Herzliya marina. Price is DM-only ('reasonable price'); a suburban Herzliya unit plausibly fits budget, but Neve Amal is not walking-distance to Herzliya's train, so TA access is bus-based.

  • Exact 6.9–5.11 window — no date negotiation needed

Afula center, Giora Yoseftal 18 — top-grade new renovation, 3 rooms (real office room), ~3,200 all-in, quiet boutique building PURSUE
Afula · מרכז העיר · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3 rooms · 60 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
₪3,000/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
The strongest listing in this cluster: a high-end fresh renovation (marble-look floors, designer bathroom) on a quiet street in the center, in a 5-unit boutique building. 3 rooms at 60sqm gives a genuine second room for the two-laptop office; brand-new furniture (double bed, wardrobe, dining set; ad adds sofa + washing machine). Rent 3,000 includes ALL bills except electricity → all-in ~3,150-3,250. Best transport in the cluster: ~13 min walk to Afula train station and ~6 min to the CBS (825 direct to TLV). Gap to close: the new kitchen has a clear ~2m counter but no visible cooktop or oven — with this prep space a portable solves it, but verify.
+
  • Highest renovation standard seen in the cluster — bright, new, genuinely inviting
  • 3 rooms: dedicated closable office room for two remote workers — rare at this price
  • 3,000 incl. all bills except electricity → ~3,150-3,250 all-in, well under budget
  • Quiet street in the center (5-resident boutique building); private ad, no broker fee
  • Best transit access: ~13 min walk to the train, ~6 min to CBS, direct 825 bus to Tel Aviv every ~30 min
  • Brand-new furniture already in place (bed, wardrobe, dining set); parking
  • ~22-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • No cooktop or oven visible in the new kitchen — cooking hardware currently unproven (prep counter is good)
  • Kitchen is a single ~2m run, compact for heavy daily cooking
  • Electricity on top — Sept AC use adds ~150-250/mo
  • 'First come wins' + available mid-July: real risk it is gone before September
  • Train to TLV still needs a Haifa transfer (~2h); the direct bus is the practical link
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability — ad pushes immediate entry, so ask early
  • Verify cooking setup: is a cooktop/oven being installed, or is a portable acceptable?
  • Confirm the sofa and remaining furniture from the ad text actually arrive
  • Confirm 'all bills except electricity' explicitly covers arnona, water AND va'ad

Afula · דרום עפולה · ₪1,640/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,500/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,640 + arnona ₪210 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Private ad, 50sqm on a quiet, leafy south-Afula street two blocks off the main drag. Photos show a complete, modest home: dining table for four, sofa + rocking chair, double bed, wardrobe, bookshelf, and crucially a desk WITH monitor and office chair already in place. Kitchen is dated but has a long granite counter (real prep space); cooking is on a double portable hotplate plus what looks like a countertop oven in the utility room, and the owner explicitly offers to complete missing equipment. All-in lands around ₪2,300 — huge budget headroom.
+

  • Real workspace already installed: desk + monitor + ergonomic chair, plus dining table as a second work spot
  • Genuinely furnished throughout (bed, wardrobe, sofa, dining set, fan, washing machine); AC listed
  • Available 2026-08-23 — perfect for a Sept 1 start
  • Quiet green residential street per ad, yet 2 min from Afula's main street; mercolet, clinic and schools nearby; ~15 min walk to the central bus station and direct 825 bus to Tel Aviv
–
  • Dated finishes: old blue-tile bathroom, worn kitchen fronts — functional, not pretty
  • No built-in stovetop/oven — cooking on portable double hotplate + countertop oven
  • 4th floor, no elevator mentioned — daily stair climb in end-of-summer heat
  • Train station is ~2.2km (not walkable daily); Tel Aviv by rail needs a transfer (~2h), bus 825 ~1h45
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (short-term not stated; available_from 23.8 suggests a standard year lease)

  • Verify elevator absence and bills ballpark (electricity with AC season)
  • Confirm the desk/monitor/office chair stay for the rental

In the photos it holds a portable double electric cooktop and a small countertop oven, but the listing only guarantees a fridge, so those cooking appliances may belong to the current tenant. Tel Hanan 3.5-room, ₪2,900 — confirmed full gas range (oven + cooktop) + granite counter, but older area and partial furnishing PURSUE
Nesher · תל חנן · ₪2,900/mo rent · 3.5 rooms · 81.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,725/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,900 + arnona ₪175 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is genuinely good and confirmed in the photos: a full white gas range (oven + 4-burner cooktop), a dark-granite counter with real prep space, a fridge, glass-front cabinets and a breakfast-bar peninsula — proper for a couple who cooks most meals. The flat is a spacious, bright 3.5-room with terrazzo floors, reasonably furnished (sofas, bed, dining table, TV console) though listed 'partial' (extra furniture on request), with two bathrooms. Downsides: Tel Hanan is the older, more working-class part of Nesher (less green/quiet than Givat Amos), it is larger than a couple needs, and several photos (a staged living room, a modern gray kitchen, two phone screenshots) are a DIFFERENT unit — the real apartment is the older-but-functional one in photos 00–04. Close to the Technion and the new Metronit line; already vacant (entry Jan 2026).
+
  • Confirmed good kitchen: full gas range (oven + 4 burners), granite counter, fridge, glass cabinets, breakfast bar
  • Cheap for the size — ₪2,900 for 3.5 rooms / 81 m²
  • Bright and renovated, terrazzo floors; three large bedrooms + two bathrooms
  • Near the Technion/university and the new Metronit BRT
  • Already vacant (entry Jan 2026) — flexible move-in
–
  • Tel Hanan is the older, less-green, more urban part of Nesher
  • Only 'partial' furnishing — extra furniture 'on request'; confirm what is actually included
  • Bigger (more rooms to heat/clean) than a couple needs
  • Photo set mixes in a different, staged unit — the pretty modern shots are NOT this flat
  • No parking noted; framed as annual/long-term
?

  • Pin down the exact furniture included and that the oven+gas kitchen shown is this unit
  • Check street-level quiet on Peretz St and walking distance to groceries without a car

Gorgeous renovated 2.5-rm in Yafo D with the best kitchen in the cluster, ~₪4,100 all-in — but the building is mid-TAMA-38 construction PURSUE
Tel Aviv · יפו ד', גבעת התמרים · ₪3,400/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 52 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,250/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona ₪200 + est. utilities ₪650 · Yad2 field: ארנונה לחודשיים: 400 (= ₪200/mo); bills not otherwise stated
confirm short-term
Beautifully styled, bright, fully furnished 52sqm 2.5-room at Hinanit 7 (Yafo D / Givat HaTmarim) for ₪3,400 + ₪200 stated arnona. The kitchen is genuinely excellent for cooks: built-in cooktop, oven, long counters PLUS a big island/bar — best cooking setup of all 9 TLV listings. Red Line stops on Jerusalem Blvd (Mahrozet/HaBesht) are ~10-12 min walk with a direct ride to central TLV and Bat Yam. The catch: the building itself is undergoing TAMA-38 works (due to finish in ~1.5 years), so weekday construction noise is a real risk for two at-home remote workers, and the landlord asks a 3-month-rent bank guarantee.
+
  • Real cook's kitchen: cooktop + oven + island + generous counters (photo-verified) — top of cluster
  • Renovated, bright, tastefully furnished; genuinely a nice place to BE
  • 2.5 rooms / 52sqm — the half-room + daybed nook give two workable laptop spots (plus room for their own desk)
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,050-4,150 (3,400 + 200 arnona + ~₪450-550 est. bills) — comfortable headroom under ₪4,500
  • ~10-12 min walk to Red Line (Mahrozet/HaBesht) on Jerusalem Blvd; quiet small residential street
  • Washing machine, oven, fridge, microwave, Tami4, sofa, TV all included
–
  • Building is mid-TAMA-38 construction (~1.5y to completion) — likely daytime drilling/noise, the main threat for two remote workers home all day
  • Requires 3 months' rent bank guarantee — heavy friction for a 2-month stay
  • Bed has no mattress ('צריך לקנות מזרן') — they'd need to buy one for a short stay
  • Yafo D is a modest working-class pocket; fewer cafes/amenities than central TLV
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability and that the landlord accepts a short let (short_term only 'likely')
  • Ask directly: is TAMA construction active on the building now? What works are scheduled Sept-Nov? Visit on a weekday morning to hear it
  • Will the landlord waive/reduce the 3-month bank guarantee for a short stay, and provide a mattress (or deduct its cost)?
  • What do bills run (electricity, water, va'ad)? Confirm arnona ₪200/mo as listed
  • Nearest supermarket walk (Yafo D / Wolfson area) for vegan+GF staples

63sqm, 3.5 rooms, fully furnished, directly opposite the beach — the kitchen window looks straight onto the sand (kitesurfers in the listing photo). The kitchen is genuinely cookable: freestanding cooker with oven, fridge, two long counter runs, cabinets, plus a dining table by the sea-view window. The catch: decor is deeply dated (floral wallpaper, heavy ornate sofas, old dressers) — clean and complete, not shabby, but far from the bright-modern ideal. 3.5 rooms mean real space for two separate work spots (small side room + living room, plus room for their own desk). Floor 4 with no elevator. All-in ≈ ₪3,125 (2,500 + stated arnona 125 + est. bills ~₪500). Quiet seaside street; Kiryat Haim rail ~15-20 min walk (or Metronit 3 / buses), TLV by train ~70-80 min; Hazorea family ~35km.

  • Quiet residential beach street; parking; 2 AC units + washer + full furniture

–

  • Floor 4 without elevator — sweaty climbs in Sept heat, hauling groceries/desk
  • 'משופץ' (renovated) claim is generous vs photos

The best fit in the cluster: 3-room 65sqm at Shai Agnon 16 (Tzur Shalom, Kiryat Bialik), ₪2,300 → all-in ≈ ₪2,900. confirm short-term
Freshly renovated, fully furnished 50sqm 2-room in Afula's center: clean white L-shaped kitchen with two real counter surfaces (fridge, cooktop, microwave — no oven), bright living room with dining table, sun balcony, parking + storage option. Rent 2,999 INCLUDING water and electricity — realistic all-in ~3,200-3,300 once arnona (est. ~200 for 50sqm, not stated) and va'ad are added. 5-6 min walk to the central bus station (direct 825 to TLV every ~30 min), ~20 min walk to the train. Private ad, no broker fee.
+
  • Bright, clean, modern and genuinely inviting — best 'nice place to BE' in the Afula batch alongside Yoseftal
  • Real L-kitchen with two work surfaces + cooktop and microwave; only the oven is missing (portable is an easy buy)
  • Water + electricity included in rent — caps utility risk during AC-heavy September
  • 5-6 min walk to Afula CBS (825 direct bus to Tel Aviv ~1h25 every ~30 min); city center, market and supermarkets walkable
  • Private ad — no brokerage fee; parking + storage
  • ~22-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • No oven; kitchen is compact overall
  • 50sqm / 2 rooms — workspace is the dining table + their own desk; no closable second room
  • Only 3 photos — bedroom and balcony unseen
  • Arnona not mentioned — assume ~200/mo on top unless included
  • Train to TLV needs a Haifa transfer (~2h) — the bus is the practical link
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (available now — will they hold it?)
  • Confirm arnona and va'ad: on top of the 2,999 or included?
  • Ask for bedroom + balcony photos and whether a portable oven is acceptable
  • Verify evening street noise (center-adjacent street)
70
HaBanim mini-penthouse Afula: homey furnished 2rm, 8th floor + elevator, ₪2,800 stated all-in — bright with views, but sits over the main junction PURSUE
Afula · ₪2,200/mo rent · 2 rooms · 200 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,800/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪200 + utilities ₪400 (stated in ad) · 2200 שכירות+ 200 ארנונה+ 150 חשמל+ 100 מים+ 150 ועד בית =2800 כולל חשבונות ומיסים
confirm short-term
Agency 2-room 'mini-penthouse' on floor 8 with elevator: the interior photos are the coziest in the cluster — two proper sofas, wood coffee table, TV console, equipped kitchen (long counter, coffee machine, kettle, toaster oven, portable hob), bedroom with double bed + dresser + shelves, renovated bathroom, plus a small furnished terrace and a huge bare 200sqm roof. Ad states 2,200+200+150+100+150 = ₪2,800 all-in. The catch: its windows overlook the big HaBanim junction/roundabout at the city's SW entrance — real road noise potential, partly offset by height and closed-window AC season.
+
  • Stated ₪2,800/mo all-in including arnona and bills — strong headroom
  • Most 'homey' furnishing seen in this cluster: real living room, double bed, equipped kitchen down to coffee machine
  • Very bright: 8th-floor panoramic light, big bedroom windows; small terrace with seating for breaks
  • Elevator + parking + pets allowed; immediate entrance
  • Kitchen counter is long with good prep space; toaster oven present
–
  • Directly above the busy HaBanim junction — street-noise risk even on floor 8; 'quiet street' it is not
  • No full stove/oven: portable hob + toaster oven only (workable given counter, but they cook seriously)
  • No dining table or desk visible — their desk covers one workstation, second spot needs the coffee table or a bought table
  • The '200sqm' is mostly an unfinished bare roof — no shade, no use in Sept heat
  • Agency ad; commission question; ~12-15 min walk to central bus station, train ~2.5km
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Visit at rush hour: how loud is the junction with windows open/closed?
  • Ask about adding a dining table and a proper cooktop
  • Confirm agency fee on a short let
68
Nesher (Ofakim) 2-room, ₪2,495 fully furnished — best value; real counter + gas cooktop but only a toaster-oven MAYBE
Nesher · ₪2,495/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 60.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,145/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,495 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a functional service-porch galley: a long dark-granite counter with good prep space, a tabletop 2-burner gas hob, a toaster-oven, microwave, fridge and washing machine by a bright window — workable for cooking, though there is no full built-in oven, which matters for a couple who bakes and roasts (and cooks vegan/GF). It is the cheapest listing and fully furnished (two sofas, bed, dining table), a right-sized 2-room (separate bedroom + living), bright with terrazzo floors, low running costs (arnona ₪0), and a 10-minute walk to the Technion. Finishes are older and it is the top floor (4/4). Photos look coherent to one apartment apart from one possibly-different bathroom.
+
  • Best value: ₪2,495, fully furnished, arnona ₪0 (low running cost)
  • Real prep space: long granite counter + gas cooktop + toaster-oven + fridge + washing machine
  • Couple-sized 2-room (separate bedroom + furnished living room)
  • Bright; 10-minute walk to the Technion
  • Move-in ready (immediate entry)
–
  • No full oven — only a tabletop 2-burner gas hob plus a toaster-oven
  • Top floor (4/4) — can be hot in September; older cabinets/finish
  • One bathroom photo looks like a different unit — verify
  • Framed as annual/long-term
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month furnished short-term let is OK (ad is annual-only)
  • Confirm the cooking setup: is the gas hob fixed, and is there any real oven?
  • Ask about summer heat/AC on the top floor and quiet on Ofakim St
68
Dream-kitchen kibbutz house with perfect Sept-Oct dates — everything hinges on the unlisted price MAYBE
Givat Haim Ihud · גבעת חיים איחוד · ₪100/mo rent · 6 rooms · 350 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪750/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪100 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Nearly-new 160sqm (built) family house in Kibbutz Givat Haim Ihud, sublet Aug 5 - Nov 2 with partial-period entry allowed — the best date fit in the cluster. Photos show a spectacular cook's kitchen (full gas cooker + large oven, dishwasher, very long counters, walls of teal cabinetry, garden view), two warm living areas, 4 bedrooms (workspace solved), and field views; washer+dryer and AC in every room. The ₪100 'price' is a placeholder — actual rent unknown, and a house like this could easily exceed the ₪4,500 all-in cap. Transport is the other catch: no walkable train (Hadera West ~5-6km), Route-4 corridor buses (e.g., 852/703) are workable but slow — realistically car life, ~1h to TLV.
+
  • Best kitchen seen in this cluster: big oven + gas hob, dishwasher, massive counter and storage — ideal for daily vegan+GF cooking
  • Beautiful, new, warm and bright house — an exceptional place to spend two indoor months
  • Dates fit perfectly: Aug 5 - Nov 2, and 'part of the period' explicitly OK
  • 4 bedrooms + two living areas = effortless double remote-work setup
  • Kibbutz quiet, fields view, parking; ~40 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea family
  • Fully equipped: washer + dryer, TVs, ceiling fans, AC everywhere
–
  • Price unknown (₪100 placeholder) — market rent for such a house could be far over budget
  • Car essentially required: train ~5-6km (Hadera West), buses to TLV slow/infrequent — Roy's weekly TLV trips ~1h+ each way
  • Groceries = kibbutz minimarket + drives to Hadera; no walkable shuk/supermarket variety
  • Far from Gedera-side family (~1h+)
  • House size means higher electricity (AC) and possibly high arnona — ask what subletter pays
?
  • FIRST QUESTION: asking price, and what it includes (arnona? water/electricity? gardening?) — if ≤ ~₪4,000-4,500 all-in this becomes a top candidate
  • Confirm a Sept-Oct (or Sept-Nov 2) 2-month slice is fine (ad says partial OK — reconfirm)
  • Any house-minding duties (garden, pets, plants)?
  • Check Route-4 bus reality at commute hours (Givat Haim junction stop) and whether they'd budget a rental car from the savings
  • Kibbutz guest/renter arrangements (gate access, use of kibbutz pool/dining if any)
68
Modern renovated 3-rooms in a quiet 2-story building (Tel Amal) — desk, dining table, built-in oven; missing only a cooktop and a stronger neighborhood MAYBE
Haifa · תל עמל · ₪2,800/mo rent · 3 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,575/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona ₪125 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
50sqm 3 small rooms at HaYarden 32, Tel Amal, private landlord, ₪2,800 + ₪125 stated arnona (~₪3,350 est. all-in). Photos show a clean modern renovation: gray corner kitchen with genuine two-run prep counter and built-in stainless oven, fridge and microwave; simple fresh bedroom; a laundry room with green tree views; glossy floors throughout. The ad's furniture list is unusually work-friendly — writing desk, expandable dining table + chairs, 2 wardrobes, AC in every room, sofa, TV. Notably absent from both list and photos: a stovetop. Building is small (2 floors) on a quiet green street — but Tel Amal belongs to the weak Halisa–Tel Amal–Neve Paz cluster and everything runs on buses.
+
  • Real kitchen prep space (corner L, two runs) + built-in oven + fridge + microwave
  • Work-ready furnishing: writing desk AND expandable dining table — two workspaces solvable in 3 rooms
  • Modern, clean, bright renovation — pleasant to spend indoor months in; AC in every room
  • Quiet: small 2-story building, trees outside, described as accessible-but-quiet area
  • ~₪3,350 est. all-in leaves ~₪1,100 headroom; arnona stated at only ₪125/mo
–
  • No stovetop listed or visible — they'd need to buy a portable hob (negotiable per their rules since prep space is real)
  • Tel Amal: socio-economically weak cluster; groceries = local minimarkets, no walkable shuk
  • Bus-dependent — no walkable train/Carmelit; Tel Aviv trips mean bus + train via downtown or Lev HaMifratz
  • 3 rooms inside 50sqm = each room is small
  • Short-term unknown — needs landlord agreement (score capped accordingly)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Confirm there is (or may be added) a cooktop; ask if landlord would supply one
  • Verify utilities ~₪400 est. (electricity/water/vaad) on top of rent+arnona
  • Check bus frequency from Yad LaBanim corridor toward Merkaz HaShmona train for the TLV commute

Yokneam · רמת יקנעם · ₪3,400/mo rent · 3 rooms · 74 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,050/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Agency 3-room, 74sqm, top floor (3/3, walkup) renovated 'from the foundation', facing the Jezreel Valley — the view photo is gorgeous and the flat is clean and simply but adequately furnished: corner sofa + recliner + TV console, wooden dining table + chairs, galley kitchen with gas hob, freestanding oven, new fridge, microwave, washer, plus a fold-out wall table. Its trump card for this couple: Yokneam is ~10 min drive from Kibbutz Hazorea (family), in a green, quiet town next to a shopping center. But at 3,400 rent + est. arnona ~₪350 + bills, all-in lands ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 — inside budget with almost no headroom, and Tel Aviv is a ~2h direct 826 bus (no train in town).
+
  • Family proximity: ~10 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea — best in cluster by far
  • Real kitchen: gas hob + oven + moderate counter + fold-out prep/eat table; new fridge
  • 3 rooms — dedicated office room for two remote workers, plus dining table
  • Renovated from scratch, bright, with open Jezreel Valley views; quiet green residential area near shopping center
  • Immediate entrance; pets allowed; building shelter
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 — nearly exhausts the ₪4,500 cap (arnona/bills estimated, not stated)
  • Tel Aviv access is the weakest here: no train in town (Kfar Yehoshua station ~12km), 826 bus ~2h; car strongly helps
  • Top-floor walkup under the roof — heat in Sept + 3 flights of stairs
  • Furnishing is functional-plain (no desk; simple pieces), less charming than the photos' view suggests
  • Agency ad; commission on a short let would break the budget
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated)
  • Get exact arnona + typical bills to nail the all-in number vs the ₪4,500 cap
  • Ask the agency about fee on 2 months — potentially decisive
  • Check roof insulation/AC coverage for the top floor in September
  • Verify Roy's realistic TLV commute (826 schedule) vs staying near Afula's train+bus hub
67
Renovated kitchen: real counter + 2 burners, no oven SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,187/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,837/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,187 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen clears their bar as a GOOD (not perfect) fit: a clean, renovated white kitchen with a genuine full-length countertop split into two usable work zones by the sink, plus a working two-burner portable hotplate already provided — real cookable prep space, exactly the "portable stovetop" they said they'd accept. The only gap is the oven (none present), which they explicitly called negotiable, and there is counter room to add a countertop oven — though the fridge looks like a small under-counter unit, a real limit for a couple cooking most meals. The rest is a compact, fully furnished but dated 1-bedroom in Wadi Salib / downtown Haifa: livable for two months bringing only a desk, with an enclosed-balcony dining nook as a second work spot. Location is the standout for a car-free couple — flat downtown, a short walk to the Haifa Center–HaShmona train (coastal line to Tel Aviv) and Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2, with the Talpiot produce/vegan shuk reachable uphill.
+
  • Renovated kitchen with a genuine full-length counter and two work zones (left + right of the sink) — real prep space, not a token ledge
  • A working two-burner portable hotplate is already provided (the exact 'portable stovetop' they said they'd accept)
  • Under budget at ~₪4,187/mo (cap ₪4,500)
  • Excellent transit for a no-car couple: short flat walk to Haifa Center–HaShmona train (coastal line to Tel Aviv) + Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2; Carmelit funicular nearby
  • Fully furnished — sofa bed, second couch, wardrobe, plus a dining nook that can double as a 2nd work spot; they only need to bring the desk
  • Talpiot Market (fresh-produce shuk with a vegan food shop) reachable; convenient to Kibbutz Hazorea family (~30–40 min)
  • Kitchen and bathroom are clean and bright
–
  • No oven at all — they'd need to buy a countertop oven (there is counter room for one)
  • Fridge appears to be a small under-counter unit — limited cold storage for a couple cooking most of their food
  • Dated, tired living areas: yellowed/aged curtains, old cork/terrazzo floors, older AC and light fixtures
  • Small apartment; the two-person work setup is cramped and the second spot is a frosted-glass enclosed-balcony nook
  • Groceries and the shuk are uphill — Haifa is steep, so it's a climb or a short Metronit/Carmelit hop from the door
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,837/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month stay in the Sept–Nov window is bookable and ask for the monthly/long-stay rate (availability listed fully open 61/61, min-nights up to 10, but a 2-month booking is unconfirmed)
  • Ask the host the fridge/freezer size and whether adding a countertop oven is OK
  • Confirm the sleeping setup — separate bedroom with a real bed, or is the sofa bed the main bed?
  • Verify street-level quiet: Wadi Salib is a redeveloping neighborhood and some blocks near the port/downtown can be noisier than the 'quiet and safe' description
  • Check GF-specific grocery availability (vegan is well covered in Haifa; gluten-free is less certain)

35sqm 2-rooms on HaTish'a St, Neve Yosef, agency ad at ₪2,200 including arnona, water and vaad (electricity extra → ~₪2,500 est. all-in). Unusually inviting for this price tier: photos show a decorated, genuinely homey flat — new light-gray sofa with plant shelves, a lovely bright bedroom with double bed and rattan pendant and green tree views, and a kitchen with a real ~2m counter, portable 2-burner hob, toaster oven, microwave, full-size fridge and washer (+dryer). 'Everything new' per ad. The gaps: no real oven/stovetop (portables only — acceptable given true prep space), no desk or dining table visible, a narrow living room, and Neve Yosef's weak bus-dependent location.
+

  • Cookable kitchen: real counter length + 2-burner hob + toaster oven + microwave + full fridge

  • No desk or dining table in photos — two-person workspace means their own desk + tight fitting in 35sqm
  • Narrow living room; couple will feel the size over 2 indoor months
  • No real oven/stovetop (portable 2-burner + toaster oven only)
  • Electricity NOT included; agency ad; short-term unknown
?

The catch is the dates: it's offered only Sept 5–25, about three weeks, not the ~2 months wanted. No price stated but the poster flags an 'especially friendly price' and Haifa rents are low, so likely within budget.

Open-plan kitchen has a decent ~2m counter run; cooktop is claimed but not visible and there is no oven. Furnished (sofa, dining table, double bed, wardrobe, fridge, washing machine, microwave, 2 ACs). One corner shows a ceiling stain — check for damp. 6-7 min walk to the CBS; agency ad, so ask about the fee.

  • Exceptional price: 2,700/mo all-in STATED in the ad, bills itemized — huge budget headroom
  • Renovated, modern-looking, fully furnished for two; large new shower room

Agency ad for a newly built yehidat diur near the city center and the college exit. Photos show everything new: freestanding gas stove WITH oven, single counter run with sink + microwave, gray sofa, small white dining table with two chairs, double bed, sliding wardrobe, stacked washer+dryer, wall TV. Rent includes arnona, va'ad and gas, plus capped water (₪100/2mo) and electricity (₪200/2mo) — realistic total ≈ ₪2,700–2,850 once two people working from home with AC blow past the tiny electricity cap. Clean and bright but genuinely small for a couple spending workdays inside.
+
  • Full real cooking: new freestanding gas hob + oven, new fridge, microwave — rare at this price
  • Effectively all-in pricing: arnona/va'ad/gas on the owner, utilities capped-then-metered; ≈ ₪2,700-2,850 realistic
  • Everything brand new and clean (first tenants were recent); Tadiran AC, bars, parking, storage
  • Central: walkable to city center shops and supermarkets; claimed quiet location
  • Immediate entrance; agency used to fast turnarounds
–
  • 38sqm / 2 small rooms — cramped for two simultaneous laptop workers; only one real work spot (small dining table)
  • Counter/prep space is moderate: one run, part occupied by microwave and kettle
  • Electricity cap of ₪200 per TWO months is unrealistically low for Sept AC use — overage on tenant, budget +₪150-250/mo
  • No pets, emphatic 'serious only' tone; agency commission likely
  • Sterile look — little warmth, curtains-closed photos make brightness unverifiable
?

Bright, fully-furnished 4-room with the best kitchen in the cluster — priciest, same border risk SKIP
Nahariya · מרכז · ₪3,500/mo rent · 4.0 rooms · 90.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,500 + arnona ₪400 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
The kitchen is excellent and exactly this couple's kind of space: a modern wood-and-white run with a built-in stainless oven, a 4-burner gas cooktop, range hood, large fridge, a decent counter and open shelving — well equipped for cooking most meals. The apartment is the brightest and most move-in-ready of the three: 90 m2, an airy furnished living/dining, furnished bedrooms, two bathrooms (one ensuite), elevator and shelter in the building, and a green park outlook a short walk from the sea, the train and the central bus station. The downsides are price (3,500, the cluster's highest, though under budget) and the same Nahariya border location, which saw rocket sirens and injuries in 2026.
+
  • Best kitchen of the three: built-in oven + 4-burner gas cooktop + range hood + large fridge + counter and open shelving
  • Fully furnished, bright and genuinely move-in ready for a 2-month stay
  • 90 m2, 3 air directions, two bathrooms (one ensuite)
  • Elevator and shelter in the building
  • Walkable to the sea, the train station and the central bus station — strong for a car-free couple
  • Green / park view, described as airy and renovated
–
  • Most expensive in the cluster at 3,500/mo (still under budget, but ~1,100/mo more than the cheapest option here)
  • Kitchen counter is a single-wall run — no large second prep surface

  • Over budget: ≈₪4,550/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)

Nesher · גבעת נשר · ₪2,500/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15

Staged, clean and genuinely inviting 45sqm 2-room on HaMeyasdim 4 (Givat Nesher), floor 1 of a small 4-tenant building, ₪2,500 + stated ₪170/mo arnona → all-in ≈ ₪3,000. The retro green-tiled kitchen is real: built-in oven, counters plus an in-kitchen dining table for extra prep space. The structural weakness is Nesher itself: no train, so Tel Aviv means bus down HaMeyasdim to Merkazit HaMifratz then train (~1h50m door-to-door), and HaMeyasdim is the neighborhood's main street — floor-1 traffic noise needs checking. Closest listing in the cluster to Kibbutz Hazorea (~20-25 min drive).

confirm short-term
Freshly renovated 8th-floor apartment with a direct Kinneret-view balcony in Migdalei HaMelachim (Gdud Barak 17), which is an 18-unit vacation-apartment building on the lakefront with 24/7 guard and parking. New furniture (some still wrapped), elegant bathroom, and a genuinely generous L-shaped kitchen counter — the best prep space in the Tiberias listings — but NO cooktop or oven is visible in any photo, and for a couple that cooks everything this must be verified before anything else. ₪2,200 rent + ₪720 vaad (includes internet/cables, cleaning, elevators, 24/7 security) + ~₪215 arnona + electricity/water ≈ ₪3,600 all-in. A 12-min walk to Tiberias CBS puts the hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m) in reach — as good as this cluster gets, though still a long haul.
+
  • Generous renovated kitchen counters — big L-shaped run, lots of cabinets — excellent prep space IF cooking appliances exist
  • All-new furniture and finishes; bright, clean, genuinely nice to be in
  • Kinneret-view balcony with seating; 8th floor lifts it above street noise
  • Vacation-apartment building = landlord accustomed to short stays — 2-month Sept-Nov deal is structurally plausible
  • Vaad ₪720 includes internet/cables, cleaning, elevator, 24/7 security and free parking; all-in ~₪3,600 leaves budget headroom
  • Best transport in the cluster: ~12 min walk to CBS, hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m); city center/shuk ~10 min walk
  • 3 rooms — small second bedroom can be a closable office for their desk

  • Gdud Barak is the main lakefront road — traffic and promenade activity below (mitigated by floor 8)
  • Tel Aviv is still 2.5h+ door-to-door each way; no train anywhere in the region
  • Tiberias at -200m is brutally hot in September — they'll live on the AC (present throughout)
  • Agency listing; no dining table visible in photos — workspace surfaces need checking

  • Ask about vacationer noise in the building during Tishrei holidays
  • Confirm arnona amount and who pays it, and typical electricity bills with AC
  • Confirm a dining/work table is (or can be) included — only a small console visible
  • Check which minimarket/supermarket is closest for their vegan/GF staples (center has minimarkets; larger supermarkets up the hill)

The kitchen is a real asset for a couple who cooks most meals: full white cabinetry, a long stone countertop with sink, a freestanding oven plus a 4-burner gas cooktop, and extra old cabinets kept in the back for heavy storage. The rest is a spacious, quiet 80 m2 older-style 4-room (terrazzo floors, big windows, 4 air directions) with a renovated bathroom and an enclosed balcony that has been used as an extra room / work corner. At 2,400 it is the cheapest and best-value unit in the cluster. The catch is the location: Nahariya sits ~10 km from Lebanon and took rocket sirens and impacts through 2026.

  • Enclosed balcony used as an extra room + work corner — helpful for two laptops
  • Public bomb shelter 15 m from the door (relevant given the border-siren risk)
  • Private covered parking under the building
  • Landlord furnishes to tenant needs (fridge, oven+gas cooktop, sofa, leather armchair, table, computer chair, double mattress, single bed, wardrobes)
–

  • Photos show a mostly empty apartment; actual furniture depends on the landlord delivering what is promised
  • No in-apartment safe room (mamad) — only the nearby public shelter
  • Ad's photo set is padded with a shared 'promo gallery' (sea view, terrazzo living rooms, artificial-grass balcony) that also appears in the other two Nahariya ads, so several images likely are NOT this unit

Entry exactly Sept 1 matches their window, and two rooms give real dual-workspace options. The math is the problem: ₪4,500 rent + stated bills of ₪250-350 (+₪25 stated arnona) lands ~₪4,775-4,875 all-in, over the cap, and this is a J-m agency ad, so a broker fee would sting badly on a 2-month stay. No cooktop or oven is visible in the photo (real counter means a portable is acceptable, but verify). Shuk-facing Agrippas means daytime market bustle and night bar noise — floor 6 and window orientation are the mitigation; light rail (Mahane Yehuda stop) is ~3 min away with Navon station ~2 stops for the 35-40 min Tel Aviv train.

Real-but-modest Hadar kitchen: no oven, tight for two to work SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,478/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,128/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,478 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is genuine but modest: a full-size Electra fridge/freezer, a real granite countertop split into two short prep runs, good cabinet storage, and a provided 2-burner portable hotplate — but no oven of any kind. That clears the "token kitchenette" bar (there's real prep space and a proper fridge), yet counter room is tight for a couple who cook most meals, and they'd have to add a countertop oven that would compete for that space. Location is a strength for cooking and car-free living: lower Hadar / Wadi Nisnas in Haifa, with the fresh-produce shuk and supermarkets walkable and Metronit/Carmelit/train links to Tel Aviv. The big drawback for these two is workspace — it's effectively one combined living/sleeping room with only a tiny table beyond the single desk they bring.
+
  • Genuine granite counter with two short prep runs + full-size fridge/freezer + a 2-burner portable hotplate already provided — real cooking is possible
  • Excellent for vegan/GF home cooking: Wadi Nisnas fresh-produce market and Hadar supermarkets within walking distance
  • Central Hadar location is car-free friendly — Metronit BRT, Carmelit funicular, and downtown train station reachable, with frequent trains/buses to Tel Aviv
  • Fully furnished for a 2-month stay (bed, dining set, TV, clothes rack, washing machine); ground floor with a small planted garden entrance
  • Price 4,478/mo within budget; Airbnb with 1-night min and full availability across the 61-day window (short-term friendly)
–
  • No oven at all — only a portable double hotplate; adding a countertop oven would eat into the already-modest prep space
  • Counter/prep space is modest (two short runs) — tight for a couple who cook most of their food
  • Weak workspace for two remote workers: just a tiny dining table plus the one desk they bring, in what looks like a single combined living/sleeping room with no separate spot
  • Basic, small and a bit dated; bathroom is worn (chipped door) and finishes are simple
  • Hadar/Wadi Nisnas is a dense, lively urban area and this is a ground-floor unit, so quiet and privacy are only moderate
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,128/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm the exact ~2-month dates within Sept-Nov are open before committing (calendar shows full availability now, but re-check)
  • Ask the host whether a small countertop oven could be added, and whether there's room for it without losing prep space
  • Verify real street-noise level (ground floor on an urban street) and how much daytime light the main room gets for working
  • Ask whether both can comfortably work/take video calls at once — is there any second usable surface?
  • Clarify whether the '2 rooms' is a true separate bedroom or one combined space (photos suggest combined)

Kiryat Ekron · קרן היסוד · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 37.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,650/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,000 + est. utilities ₪650
car recommendedconfirm short-term
KITCHEN per the listing is a gas cooktop plus a toaster oven (no full built-in oven) and a fridge — workable for a couple who cooks, but the no-oven limitation matters for vegan/GF baking and roasting, and no reliable photo actually shows this unit's kitchen (the modern white kitchen shot is a recycled cross-listing image, and a black rooftop kitchen shot is a different unit entirely). What is genuinely shown is a recently renovated 37m² unit with fresh (if garish) marble-panel walls, glossy floors, a bedroom with a 4-door wardrobe, and a furnished living room. Its real appeal is price and location: ₪3,000 all-inclusive is the cheapest here and Kiryat Ekron is a quiet small town ~10 min from the Gedera family.
+
  • ₪3,000 including arnona — by far the cheapest in the cluster (cheaper is better for this couple)
  • Very close to the Gedera family (~10 min) and adjacent to Rehovot (~10 min) — quiet small-town setting they'd value
  • Fully furnished (sofa, table + chairs, bed + 4-door closet, fridge) and recently renovated
  • Has a real gas cooktop for daily cooking
–
  • No full oven — only a toaster oven; a real limitation for a couple who cooks most meals and may bake
  • This unit's actual kitchen is not reliably shown in any photo (several photos are recycled/other-unit carousel images), so counter/prep space is unknown
  • Smallest unit (37m²); tight for two people working from laptops, no desk shown
  • Kiryat Ekron has no train of its own — transit runs via Rehovot; more car-dependent with fewer specialty (vegan/GF) grocery options in town
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) short-term let is possible — listing is annual-oriented
  • Ask for real photos of THIS kitchen and confirm counter space, the cooktop, and whether any oven is available
  • Given a possible no-car scenario, check bus/train connections to Rehovot and grocery options within walking distance
  • Confirm what furniture actually stays and the condition (photo set mixes several different units)
60
₪2,200 ALL-IN renovated Wadi Nisnas studio with loft — unbeatable cost/location, but tiny for two remote workers MAYBE
Haifa · ואדי נסנאס · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪2,200/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,200 · המחיר 2200 ש"ח כולל מיסים מים וחשמל
confirm short-term
Renovated ~30sqm one-room + sleeping gallery on Shivat Tzion, on the edge of Wadi Nisnas. Photos show a clean white studio: gray sofa, small round table, wardrobe, loft mattress, and a kitchenette with sink, portable electric double hob, small under-counter oven, mini-fridge and microwave. Location is the star: Wadi Nisnas market (superb produce/legumes/tahini for vegan-GF cooking, open Saturdays) is steps away and Merkaz HaShmona train station is ~11 min on foot — the best train access in this cluster for Roy's Tel Aviv weeks. But one small counter section and a mini-fridge make heavy daily cooking for two a squeeze, and two laptop workstations in 30sqm is tight.
+
  • ₪2,200 includes arnona, water AND electricity — cheapest real all-in in the cluster, ~₪2,300/mo headroom (funds co-working, which fixes the workspace problem)
  • ~11 min walk to Merkaz HaShmona train station (direct trains to Tel Aviv ~1h) plus Metronit and many bus lines nearby
  • Wadi Nisnas market around the corner — outstanding fresh produce for vegan/GF cooking
  • Renovated, clean, bright white finish; furnished (sofa, wardrobe, small table, loft bed); AC
  • Ad says long-term possible — flexible landlord, short stint looks feasible (available since 1.7)
–
  • 30sqm studio for a couple who are both home-working and home-cooking most meals — very tight
  • Kitchenette: one short counter run, portable hob and small oven, mini-fridge — cookable but cramped for 'most meals' cooking
  • Sleeping is a loft mattress up a ladder — fine for some, tiresome for two months
  • Shivat Tzion is a through-street with bus traffic — expect street noise on floor 1
  • Listing photos appear to mix two similar kitchen setups — verify what the actual unit contains
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (ad implies flexibility but doesn't state it)
  • Confirm fridge size and whether the under-counter oven seen in photos is actually in this unit (ad lists only electric hob, fridge, microwave)
  • Ask which floor/side the window faces — street side vs. back matters a lot for noise here
  • Check ceiling height under/over the gallery for comfortable desk placement

Spacious, clean studio on HaShomer 30 (Yalag, lower Hadar), 3rd floor, two aspects and a green/bay-glimpse view. The kitchen alcove (windowed ex-balcony) has an L-shaped tiled counter — genuine prep space — plus tall fridge-freezer, microwave and washer; cooking is on a stated electric hotplate, no oven, no built-in hob. By their own rule that's negotiable since prep space is real. Furniture includes a work desk + chair (stated) and a small round table → two work spots feasible. Talpiot market is 2-3 minutes away (produce paradise for vegan/GF), Metronit 3/5 stops nearby, train ~25 min downhill walk. The block is gritty market-adjacent Hadar. All-in ≈ ₪2,400-2,500 (1,800 + est. arnona ~₪150-180 + bills ~₪450). Not a duplicate of the Shapira studio (photo-checked).

  • Work desk + chair stated, plus side table → both can work

  • Gritty Yalag/market block — daytime bustle, tired surroundings
  • 1.5 rooms: both working all day in one space
  • Checks required; private landlord, short-term unknown
  • Train is a 25-min downhill walk (uphill home)
?

Photos: clean gray-tile living room with sofa, ~2m kitchen counter with mosaic backsplash and a black portable 2-burner hob (no oven), renovated bathroom, bedroom with double bed + large wardrobe + ceiling fan. Otherwise it is an appealing ground-floor unit explicitly aimed at a couple: a private separate entrance onto a patio, quiet, bright and genuinely furnished (sofa, dining table with chairs, a made-up bed, fridge).   • Bright and freshly renovated
  • Good black-marble counter run and a double sink

  • Most expensive of the four at ₪4,000

  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)

rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪244 + est. utilities ₪650

However no built-in cooktop or oven is clearly visible in the photos (only a countertop toaster-oven sits on the washing machine), and the ad says most furnishings/appliances are 'negotiable with the current tenant' — so what stays is a real question. The rest of the flat is lived-in and worn (older bathroom, some ceiling staining, cluttered) but it is roomier (63 m², 2.5 rooms), cheapest at 4,000, and the living room already has a desk-with-monitor workspace.

  • Dated and worn — old bathroom with staining, cluttered/occupied, only partially furnished (2 closets, dresser, table)
  • No parking spot (fine without a car, hard if they get one); 3rd of 4 floors, elevator unconfirmed
  • Far from family in both Gedera (south) and Hazorea (north) — long drive/train either way
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,894/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?

landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
55sqm 2-room at Herzfeld St (Nir Aviv, SE Tel Aviv near Derech HaTayasim and Ramat Gan National Park), renovated to a genuinely inviting standard: big bright living room, two sofas, good bedroom. Contract price ₪4,500 including everything except electricity. In-unit kitchen is only a kitchenette (small counter, mini-fridge, countertop oven, no proper cooktop visible), but the ad offers a 'huge additional kitchen' for +₪100/mo — the photo shows a full real kitchen (built-in oven, long counters, full fridge). With electricity (~₪250-350, two ACs in Sept heat) + the kitchen option, all-in lands ≈ ₪4,850-4,950 — ~8-10% over the ₪4,500 cap, which weighs the score down.
+

  • ₪4,500 stated to include rent + arnona + water + va'ad (all but electricity) — cost certainty

?

Gorgeous new Aniam (Golan) cabin unit, ~₪3,500 nearly all-in — but car-dependent and in the Golan, so capped at maybe MAYBE
Aniam · אניעם · ₪3,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 200 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,300/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,300 · כולל חשמל עד 500 שח לחודשיים, כולל אינטרנט, מים... כולל ארנונה. לא כולל מיסי מושב
confirm short-term
Brand-new, genuinely beautiful wood-ceiling unit on a quiet Golan moshav 8 min from Katzrin: fully furnished (corner sofa, dining table, quality double bedroom), AC throughout, and rent includes arnona, water, internet and electricity up to ₪500/2mo — roughly ₪3,450-3,650 all-in with moshav fees. Kitchen has an induction cooktop, hood, fridge and a real counter run but no oven visible or mentioned. The dealbreakers for this couple: transport to Tel Aviv is effectively car-only (~2.5h drive; bus = Katzrin 843 once a day ~3h15m or via Tiberias ~4h), and it sits ~12 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line — mid-2026 is calm with US-brokered security talks ongoing, but the front is unresolved (IDF active in the buffer zone; nearby Katzrin took direct rocket hits in 2024), which collides with their 'not near borders' preference.
+
  • Condition is exceptional: new, bright, inviting — easily the nicest interior in this cluster; explicitly pitched at a couple
  • Near all-in pricing: arnona, water, internet included + electricity up to ₪500/2 months — ~₪3,450-3,650/mo total, under budget
  • Fully furnished: corner sofa + TV, dining table, double bed + wardrobe, outdoor dining porch, AC in every room
  • Very quiet moshav setting; Golan elevation (~400m) is meaningfully cooler than Tiberias in Sept heat
  • Aniam has an artists' village with cafes and a small makolet; Katzrin's commercial center + supermarket 8 min drive
  • Available Aug 5 — fits a Sept start
–
  • Golan Heights, ~12 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line: calm in mid-2026 and de-escalation talks are advancing, but the situation is unresolved (IDF operating in the buffer zone, Feb 2026 Quneitra incursion) — conflicts with the couple's 'not near borders' preference, so capped at maybe
  • Transport to Tel Aviv is very poor: no direct service; Katzrin's single daily 843 bus (~04:50, ~3h15m) or ~4h via Tiberias — Roy's weekly TLV routine essentially requires a car
  • No oven visible or mentioned (induction cooktop only); counter is decent but the kitchen is compact
  • Only one closable room — two-laptop workspace means dining table + porch, no separate office
  • Moshav fees (מיסי מושב) explicitly NOT included and amount unstated
  • No pets allowed (not relevant unless they get one); electricity beyond ₪500/2mo on tenant
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term is 'likely', not explicit)
  • Ask the moshav fee amount per month (מיסי מושב)
  • Ask if an oven exists or if the landlord would allow/add a portable convection oven; confirm counter/prep space in person
  • Ask typical electricity use vs the ₪500/2mo cap (Sept AC)
  • Reality-check the car question: without a car this location doesn't work for weekly TLV trips
58
HaVered 14 Migdal HaEmek: the best REAL kitchen in the cluster (built-in oven + gas hob) at ~₪2,900 all-in — but the ad wants long-term tenants MAYBE
Migdal HaEmek · הורד · ₪2,300/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Private 2-room, 45sqm, 1st floor in the HaVered quarter. Photos show a genuinely cookable kitchen — built-in gas hob AND built-in oven, L-shaped wood-cabinet counter with fair prep space, retro fridge — plus a bright green-view living room with sofa and armchairs, double-ish bed with large sliding wardrobe (somewhat cluttered/lived-in), separate shower and WC (dated but fine). The blocker is explicit: 'מיועדת לשכירות טווח ארוך' — the owner wants long-term, so a 2-month Sept–Nov window is a long shot. Costs ≈ ₪2,900 all-in (2,300 + est. arnona ~₪170 + est. bills). Migdal HaEmek has the direct 826 bus to Tel Aviv and a rail station 4km south (Superbus 6/9 shuttle); Hazorea is ~20 min by car.
+
  • Only listing here with a fully built-in oven + gas hob — real cooking out of the box
  • ≈ ₪2,900/mo all-in estimate — comfortable headroom
  • Bright living room with garden/green views; complete furnishing (sofa, armchairs, bed, big wardrobe)
  • Quiet location with good neighbors per ad; bus stops and commercial center close by
  • Private landlord, no agency fee; ~20 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea (family)
–
  • Ad explicitly targets LONG-TERM rental — 2-month agreement unlikely
  • Lived-in and a bit worn/cluttered (fabric drawers, older bathroom) — functional, below the renovated options in feel
  • 45sqm/2 rooms: workable but no dedicated office room; one work spot + room for their desk
  • No train in town (station 4km, shuttle bus); TLV ~2h by 826 bus or bus+rail combo
  • Arnona and bills not stated — estimated from town+size
?
  • Ask anyway about a ~2-month Sept–Nov let — the ad says long-term, so get an explicit yes/no before investing more
  • Verify bed size (photo shows a narrow-ish metal frame)
  • Confirm arnona (₪170/mo is an estimate for 45sqm Migdal HaEmek)
  • Check exact bus options to Tel Aviv and to the rail station from HaVered
58
HaHarish St Afula: top-grade fresh renovation at ₪3,400 stated all-in — but photographed empty and the promised furniture list has no sofa or table MAYBE
Afula · שכון עובדים · ₪2,850/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,850 + arnona ₪250 + utilities ₪300 (stated in ad) · (2850+ 150חשמל+ 250 ארנונה+ 100מים+50ועד-בית = 3400 מיסים וריהוט)
confirm short-term
Agency 3-room, 70sqm, 3rd floor in Shikun Ovdim (quiet residential quarter NW of the center). March photos show a just-finished, genuinely high-end renovation — black kitchen with white quartz counter, new bathroom with double shower — but the flat is completely empty. Ad text promises: fridge, washer, microwave, hob, double bed + mattress, wardrobe, 3 ACs, and states 2,850+150+250+100+50 = ₪3,400 including bills. No oven, and pointedly no sofa, dining table or desk in the list — for two homeworkers that means buying/bringing the living layer. Not a duplicate of the Wolfson 3-room or Yoseftal 18 (different units verified by photos).
+
  • Stated ₪3,400/mo all-in including arnona and bills — clear, inside budget
  • Immaculate new renovation: quality tiling, new bathroom, new kitchen with quartz counter (decent prep space both sides of sink)
  • 3 rooms / 70sqm — separate office room possible
  • Quiet residential neighborhood, ~15 min walk to center; 3 ACs promised
  • Immediate entrance
–
  • Photos show an EMPTY flat; furnishing exists only as a text promise — and the promised list omits sofa, dining table, desk
  • No oven (only כיריים) — portable oven needed
  • Listing dates to March 2026 — state and availability may have changed
  • Agency ad; commission question
  • ~1.7km to the train station; TLV rail needs transfer
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability (not stated; ad is stale from March)
  • Ask for CURRENT photos — what furniture is actually in place now, especially sofa/table
  • Confirm the ₪3,400 all-in figure still holds and covers everything
  • Ask about adding an oven
58
All-new Ramat Tivon unit, two balconies to greenery, most bills included — near Hazorea MAYBE

  • Town fit: pastoral, quiet, ~10 min from Kibbutz Hazorea

Modern 14th-floor 3-room in the lower city with sea view — kitchen underdocumented, at budget ceiling SKIP
Haifa · עיר תחתית · ₪4,500/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 58.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14

rent ₪4,500 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term

Otherwise it's an appealing modern unit — a new, well-kept high-rise, floor 14 with a sea view, clean furnished living/dining and bedrooms, near the train and Metronit and a strip of cafes/restaurants. Priced at the ₪4,500 ceiling plus ~₪250/mo arnona.
+
  • Modern, clean, new building; quiet high floor with a sea view
  • Good transit: near lower-city train stations and Metronit, lots of eateries nearby
  • Furnished with dining table+4, sofa, two beds, wardrobes; lobby laundry in building
–
  • Cooktop but NO oven, and the actual kitchen/prep space isn't shown — real cooking capacity unproven
  • At the ₪4,500 budget ceiling AND ~₪500/2-months arnona → effective cost over budget
  • No parking; only 58 m²
  • Montage photos make the true finish level hard to judge
?

  • Ask whether the sea-view unit shown is truly the one for rent

Spacious dated 3-room in Motzkin's Avivim by the Kiryon - but the kitchen is barely shown MAYBE

What is clear: 80 m2 / 3 rooms, empty and clean, with two updated shower bathrooms and a separate WC, in Avivim right by the Kiryon mall, Metronit and buses (strong for groceries and car-free living). But it is dated (terrazzo, old kitchen), only partially furnished, and at 3,400 it is near the top of budget.

  • 80 m2, 3 rooms - spacious

  • Only partially furnished - would need more than a desk to live in
  • Dated terrazzo throughout
  • 3,400/mo - upper end of budget
  • Photos 08-18 are 'similar listings' contamination; only 00-07 are this unit

Givat Olga seaside studio, ₪2,800 — cooktop + counter, no oven, best TA commute MAYBE
Hadera · גבעת אולגה · ₪2,800/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 30.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,450/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,800 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Kitchen is a real L-shaped fitted counter with a sink, wooden base cabinets, and an electric cooktop — genuine prep space and a working stovetop, but no oven, which matters for a couple who cook most meals. It's a compact 30 m2 furnished studio (double bed, wardrobe, wicker sitting corner, fridge) ~100 m from the sea on a quiet street with a private yard. Location is the strongest asset: Givat Olga sits near Hadera-West station for a 45-55 min TA train.
+
  • Working electric cooktop plus a real L-shaped counter/sink for prep — usable for daily cooking
  • Best public-transport location of the cluster: near Hadera-West station, ~45-55 min to Tel Aviv
  • ~100 m from the beach, quiet street, private yard; fully furnished and move-in ready
  • Cheap at ₪2,800; low/zero arnona noted

  • No oven — limits baking/roasting for a couple cooking most meals
  • Only 30 m2 studio (1.5 rooms) — tight for two people living and both working from laptops for two months
  • No desk/dedicated workspace shown; two remote workers would share one small table
  • Kitchen and finishes look somewhat dated; some listing photos appear to be of other units
?

  • Verify walkable vegan/GF groceries in Givat Olga and the walk/bus time to Hadera-West station (no car)

Larger dated ground-floor 2.5-room with a yard (Nachal Shefaram) - worn, cramped kitchen MAYBE

rent ₪2,700 + arnona ₪300 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term

The upside is space and outdoors: 68 m2 on the ground floor with a private yard, in a newer-built pocket of Kiryat Bialik the listing calls central and transit-convenient. But finishes are tired (pink dated bathtub, worn floors) and at 2,700 it is pricier than the better-kept cheaper units.

  • Tired finishes (pink dated bathroom, scuffed floors)
