Best kitchen and condition of the cluster — but essentially unfurnished and annual-only MAYBE
Kiryat Ata · קרית בנימינה · ₪3,300/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,210/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,300 + arnona ₪260 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is the strongest in the cluster: a long gray galley with a freestanding gas stove/oven, a generous counter run and lots of cabinets — excellent for a couple who cook most meals. The apartment is beautifully renovated (high-end marble bathroom, gray-marble floors, bright) in the quiet Kiryat Binyamin neighborhood. The problem for a 2-month stay is fit: the real photos show it largely empty (the listed furniture is only fridge/oven/closet — no bed, sofa or dining set), and it is annual-only with entry 1/1/2026 (12 payments). The listing also reuses staged/stock photos from other units.
+
  • Excellent kitchen — oven + gas cooktop + long counter + ample cabinets (best of the four)
  • High-end renovation: marble bathroom, gray-marble floors, bright throughout
  • Quiet residential Kiryat Binyamin neighborhood (near Kiryat Binyamin Park)
  • Within budget (₪3,300)
–
  • Essentially unfurnished (only fridge/oven/closet listed; real photos show empty rooms) — needs a bed, sofa and table for a 2-month stay
  • Annual-only lease, entry 1/1/2026 — both term and timing clash with a Sept-Nov ~2-month need
  • Floor 4 of 4 — elevator presence uncertain
  • Listing padded with stock/other-unit photos (sea view, wooden-island kitchen, etc. do not match)
  • Kiryat Ata has no train; Tel Aviv ~1.5h+ (minor for remote workers)
?
  • Confirm whether any short-term (~2-month) option exists at all given the annual framing
  • Confirm what furniture, if any, is actually included
  • Verify move-in timing (listing says 1/1/2026) against their Sept-Nov window
  • Ask about elevator access on the top floor
  • Check car-free groceries and family access to Kibbutz Hazorea
56
Quirky multi-level Nachlaot studio with a real (small) kitchen, steps from the shuk SKIP
Jerusalem · נחלאות · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 22.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪4,650/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-termvery small
The kitchen is the best in this cluster and cooking here is realistically possible: a genuine compact L-shaped kitchen with a sink, two runs of counter, upper and lower cabinets, a cooktop and a mini-fridge (no oven). It's still small and oven-less, so gluten-free baking/roasting is out and two people prepping at once will bump elbows, but daily vegan cooking is workable. The catch is the unit itself: 22m2 spread over three levels connected by internal stone stairs, partly below ground (dim, small/glass-block windows, a portable dehumidifier in shot), and the owner explicitly says it suits a single person. Location is excellent for a car-free couple - Shabazi St in the heart of Nachlaot, a short walk to Mahane Yehuda shuk (ideal for vegan/GF fresh produce), Sacker Park, the light rail and city center.
+
  • Best kitchen of the cluster: real cabinets, L-shaped counter, cooktop, mini-fridge
  • Fully furnished incl. sofa-bed, dining table, wardrobe, washer AND dryer, AC
  • Prime walkable Nachlaot location, minutes from Mahane Yehuda shuk (vegan/GF friendly)
  • Price ₪4,000 includes arnona (municipal tax) and water; under budget
  • Multi-level layout gives some separation between living, kitchen and sleeping nooks
–
  • No oven - a real limit for a GF/vegan couple who cook most meals
  • Only 22m2 and owner states it 'suits a single person' - tight for two
  • Partly underground, small/glass-block windows, likely dim; dehumidifier suggests humidity
  • Three levels with lots of internal stairs; no proper desk - two laptops would be cramped
  • Older, worn finishes (dated bathroom) vs. the renovated look of the other listing
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability - listing says immediate entry / 12 payments a year, so short-term is unconfirmed
  • Ask about natural light, damp/humidity and whether the AC/dehumidifier runs constantly
  • Ask whether two people can realistically live and both work remotely here
  • Check for any oven or countertop oven, and total usable counter space

Migdal HaEmek, HaTimorim 19 — dated but complete: real kitchen with full gas oven, furnished, elevator, ~3,300 all-in MAYBE

Separate dining room doubles as a two-person office. Elevator building at the quiet forest edge. All-in ~3,250-3,300. It loses points on aesthetics: worn wallpaper, dated bathroom — livable, not inviting.

Or Akiva 3-room, ₪3,000 — flexible 6-month terms, but unreliable photos MAYBE
Or Akiva · קנדי · ₪3,000/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 59.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,870/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,000 + arnona ₪220 + est. utilities ₪650
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Kitchen can't be judged confidently: the ad text doesn't describe it, and the photo set is inconsistent — one image is a bright white fitted kitchen with a ceramic cooktop AND a built-in oven, but another photo is identical to the more expensive 968c... listing's living room, so it's unclear which kitchen actually belongs to this unit. On paper it's an appealing option — 59 m2 / 3 rooms, quiet and central, ₪3,000, and notably advertised on 6-month (not annual) terms, which is the friendliest short-term signal in the cluster. Furniture, though, is minimal (AC, wall closet, a table), so it may not be truly furnished for a 2-month couple.
+
  • 6-month lease terms advertised — most short-term-friendly listing in the cluster
  • Roomier than the studios (59 m2 / 3 rooms) and cheaper than the 4-room at ₪3,000
  • Described as central and very quiet; family in Kibbutz Hazorea ~30-40 min away

  • Photo set is unreliable — shares an identical living-room/kitchen image with listing 968c... (different seller/address/price), so the real kitchen is unverified
  • Ad lists only minimal furniture (AC, closet, table); likely NOT furnished enough for a 2-month stay without adding items
  • Or Akiva has no train station; Binyamina (~40-55 min to TA) needs a bus/ride — awkward with no car
  • Kitchen not described in the listing text at all
?
  • Confirm the ~2-month short-term let — 6-month terms are a good start but still longer than needed
  • Get current photos of THIS unit's actual kitchen (cooktop + oven?) — do not trust the shared/mismatched images
  • Clarify what furniture is actually included; a furnished 2-month stay likely needs more than AC/closet/table

Rishon LeZion Neve Chof — bright renovated garden unit, but only a small kitchenette SKIP
Rishon LeZion · נווה חוף · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 50.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
KITCHEN is the weak point: a compact one-wall kitchenette with taupe cabinets, a stainless sink, a modest counter and a full-height fridge, but no oven or stovetop visible in the photos — limited prep space for a couple who cooks most meals. Otherwise it is the nicest-feeling unit after Ramla: freshly renovated and bright, set in a lush garden, with a well-furnished bedroom that has a built-in desk niche (a real workspace for one laptop), a living room with a sofa, and an enclosed sun-porch entry. It sits at the top of the budget and is explicitly advertised as long-term.
+
  • Renovated, bright and airy with a green garden outlook; two air directions
  • Built-in desk niche in the bedroom — a genuine work surface for one laptop
  • Well furnished (living room, bedroom, kitchen, balcony) and clean condition
  • Rishon LeZion has train + an incoming light rail → Tel Aviv ~30–40 min; residential/quiet
–
  • Kitchen is a small kitchenette with limited counter and no visible oven/stovetop — against this couple's #1 priority
  • ₪4,500 — top of budget
  • Explicitly 'long-term contract' and no smokers — a ~2-month let may be a hard sell
  • Further from the Gedera family than the Rehovot/Kiryat Ekron options; only one modest work surface for two remote workers
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) stay is even possible — the ad states long-term, so this is the first thing to clear
  • Ask whether the kitchenette has a stovetop and/or oven, or only a sink + fridge
  • Check the second-laptop workspace situation (only one built-in desk shown)
  • Confirm the electricity-by-meter cost on top of the otherwise all-inclusive rent

Explicit month-flexible sublet in quiet Ramat Aviv G, ₪4,490 truly all-in — zero photos, kitchen unverifiable MAYBE
Tel Aviv · רמת אביב ג' · ₪3,900/mo rent · 1 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
₪3,900/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
no photos
25sqm studio sublet at Shlomo Ben Yosef 27 (Ramat Aviv G), 7th floor, 'fully equipped after thorough renovation', entry Aug 1 'for a month or months' — the cleanest short-term fit in the cluster on paper. ₪3,900 + ₪590 flat for ALL bills = ₪4,490 all-in, just under cap with total cost certainty. But the ad has no photos (video sent privately), so the #1 criterion — a cookable kitchen with real counter space — is completely unverified, and 25sqm is tight for two remote workers.
+
  • Sublet explicitly open to 'a month or months' — no landlord-agreement gymnastics
  • True all-in pricing: ₪3,900 + ₪590 'הכל כולל הכל' = ₪4,490, under cap with no bill surprises
  • Ramat Aviv G: quiet, green, safe; supermarket at the neighborhood center; 7th floor = airy and bright as described
  • Parking included (useful if they rent a car with budget headroom)
  • Renovated recently per ad
–
  • No photos at all — kitchen, condition, furniture and workspace all unverified (ad promises a video on request)
  • 25sqm studio is cramped for a couple working from home on two laptops
  • North-TLV location is bus-dependent: no light-rail; TAU train station ~2.5km; ~25-35 min by bus to central TLV
  • At ₪4,490 all-in there is almost no price headroom under the cap
?
  • Ask for the video + stills, kitchen first: oven? cooktop? counter length? — this decides everything
  • Confirm Sept-Nov window works (entry listed Aug 1 — is it still free for September start?)
  • Confirm the ₪590 covers arnona too, and that 'הכל כולל הכל' includes internet
  • Can a second small desk fit? Ask for a floor plan / sqm layout
  • Confirm washing machine presence

rent ₪2,150 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
no photos
Sublet-board listing (short-term native) at ₪2,150/mo for a furnished, 'new, bright, ventilated' 2-room unit on Amos St, Hadar Merkaz. Location is genuinely convenient: Carmelit HaNevi'im ~7 min, Talpiot market ~8 min, Merkaz HaShmona train ~15 min downhill, Metronit 5 through lower Hadar. But there are NO photos, so the couple's #1 criterion (a real cookable kitchen) is unverifiable, and the ad's '5 minutes from Technion' line contradicts the Hadar address — template/repost noise. Estimated all-in ≈ ₪2,750-2,850 (rent + est. arnona ~₪130-200 + bills ~₪450).
+
  • Sublet — short-term is native, no landlord-agreement gamble
  • ₪2,150 rent → est. all-in ~₪2.8k, big headroom under the ₪4.5k cap
  • Central Hadar transit: Carmelit + Metronit + walkable train (Merkaz HaShmona ~1km)
  • Talpiot market walkable — cheap produce for vegan/GF cooking
  • Claimed new, furnished, bright, with AC/balcony/parking flags set
–
  • No photos at all — kitchen, counters, condition, workspace all unknown
  • '5 min from Technion' doesn't match Amos 1, Hadar — sloppy or recycled ad
  • Available from June; Sept-Nov window not stated
  • Floor 1 with bars in dense Hadar core — street noise/privacy unknown
  • 70sqm claim for a 2-room yehidat diur looks inflated
?
  • Ask for photos or an early visit — kitchen (oven/stove/counter) first, then workspace spots
  • Confirm the unit still runs as a sublet for ~2 months inside Sept-Nov 2026 (ad posted for June)
  • Are bills/arnona included in the ₪2,150? Get the real all-in number
  • Clarify the actual address (Amos 1 vs the Technion claim) before traveling to view

12-min walk to Nahariya's train terminus and a real kitchen — capped by the Lebanon-border security picture MAYBE
Nahariya · ₪2,499/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,374/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,499 + arnona ₪225 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
Clean, freshly painted 70 sqm 3-room on Kibbutz Galuyot St: real kitchen (gas range visible, ~2m stone counter), double bed and wardrobes, glass dining table, and a spare empty room that could take their desk. Nahariya is the cluster's transit king — ~1.1km walk to the terminus with direct Tel Aviv trains ~1h40 — and shops are walkable. But Nahariya sits ~9km from the Lebanon border: the March–June 2026 war put rocket barrages over the city (hospital moved underground, beaches closed) and the June 26 framework agreement remains fragile, so per the standing rule this is capped at 'maybe' pending a security re-check near September.
+
  • Best transport in the cluster: ~12 min walk to Nahariya station, direct TLV trains ~1h40
  • Real kitchen: gas range visible plus a decent stone counter
  • Clean, freshly painted, immediate entry
  • Walkable supermarkets, shops and services (city center ~700m)
  • Spare empty room = easy second workspace for their own desk
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,225 (arnona stated ₪225/mo)
–
  • Security cap: ~9km from the Lebanon border; rocket barrages on Nahariya as recently as May–June 2026, ceasefire fragile and rejected by Hezbollah
  • Only partially furnished: living room sparse, one room empty, no sofa visible
  • Floor contradiction: ad text says floor 2, listing field says floor 4; elevator unknown
  • Agency ad, short-term unknown
  • Kitchen counter modest for heavy two-person cooking
?
  • Re-verify the northern security situation before committing — this is the gating item for Nahariya
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability with the landlord
  • Clarify floor (2 vs 4) and whether there is an elevator
  • Exact furniture list — is there a sofa, and can a desk fit in the empty room?
  • Homeless ad 3ba205c32528b2d8 shows a very similar flat (same street/specs/price) with a different kitchen finish — possibly the same building or this unit pre-renovation; cross-check when contacting

HaMaccabim 10: grandma-style but ₪2,500 INCLUDING arnona, water and vaad — cheapest all-in MAYBE

~₪2,500/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪2,500 · המחיר 2500 כולל ארנונה ומים וועד בית למעט החשמל — rent includes arnona, water and vaad; electricity excluded
confirm short-term
Time-capsule 3-room (65sqm) in Givat HaRakafot, Kiryat Ata: ₪2,500 including arnona, water and vaad bayit — only electricity on top, so all-in ≈ ₪2,700-2,750, the cheapest in the cluster by far. Fully furnished (oven, gas, fridge, dining set, sofa) with a real retro-pink kitchen with decent counters, entrance is just 4 steps up, quiet surroundings near a commercial center and bus stop, and the dining nook has an open view toward the bay. The catch: heavy grandma decor and photos taken mid-occupancy with clutter everywhere — 'a nice place to BE' is doubtful until seen empty.
+
  • Cheapest all-in in cluster: ≈ ₪2,700-2,750 (rent includes arnona + water + vaad; only electricity extra)
  • Real kitchen: oven, gas, fridge stated; retro but decent counter run and storage
  • Fully furnished (dining table, sofa, beds, dresser) — and can even be emptied if preferred
  • Quiet surroundings (stated), commercial center + bus stop + abundant parking nearby
  • Practically ground floor (4 steps) with shelter behind the building
  • Open view over the bay from the dining nook
–
  • Very dated ornate interior (chandeliers, floral sofas) — far from the bright/clean vibe they want
  • Photos show the current occupant's heavy clutter — hard to judge condition underneath
  • No train in Kiryat Ata — TLV ~1h45m-2h; workspace ergonomics unclear (vanity/dining tables only)
  • Religious landlord ('not on Shabbat') may mean stricter house norms
?
  • Confirm landlord agrees to ~2-month Sept-Nov stay (short_term unknown)
  • Ask to view AFTER current tenant clears out — condition and light without the clutter
  • Confirm floor situation (ad says first floor/4 steps; listing metadata says floor 2)
  • Where do two laptop workstations go? Check the dining table + a desk spot
  • Do not call on Shabbat (stated in ad)

~₪3,700/mo all-inrent only

confirm short-term
65sqm 3-rooms one building up from the Masada cafe strip, private landlord, ₪2,700 + ₪350 stated arnona (~₪3,500 est. all-in with utilities). The ad's inventory is exactly what they need — fridge, oven (photos show a freestanding 4-burner electric stove+oven), washing machine, kitchen table, double bed, wardrobe, dresser and a writing desk — and 3 rooms means two real workspaces. The catch is condition: photos show badly peeling paint in the bedroom and second room, a very old kitchen with minimal counter, and vintage-worn floors. Location is genuinely excellent (Carmelit Masada station, Talpiot market, cafes below); the flat itself is the weak link.
+
  • REAL cooking setup confirmed: freestanding stove + oven, fridge, washing machine, kitchen table
  • 3 rooms / 65sqm — two separate workspaces incl. a stated writing desk

Agency ad, 72sqm renovated 3-room near the Wolfson/south-Afula shderot. Photos show fresh paint and floors and lots of light, but furnishing is skeletal: one navy loveseat, white kitchen run with a portable 2-burner hob, fridge, wardrobe, washing machine — no dining table, no oven, beds claimed in text but rooms photograph empty. For a couple that owns only a desk and needs a turn-key 2-month home, the gaps are real friction. All-in ≈ ₪3,500 (2,800 + 250 stated arnona + est. bills), which buys much less here than Koresh St's ₪2,500 package.

rent ₪2,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Honest private ad in upper Tiberias (Jabotinsky 2, Shikun Dalet): partially renovated ('the renovation is partial' per the seller), tidy kitchen with a freestanding gas cooker (stovetop + real oven), a workable single-run counter, microwave and fridge — a genuinely cookable kitchen at ~₪2,850/mo all-in. It's steps from the Yesh Hesed supermarket and the Big Tiberias center, with a heavily-served bus stop nearby. The catches: the flat fronts HaNasi Weizmann, Tiberias' main upper artery (ad claims new noise-insulating doors), the ad promises only partial furniture (two single beds + wardrobe + table and chairs — sofa and the photographed desk may leave with the tenant), and the WC shows patched, unpainted wall damage.
+
  • Real cooking kitchen confirmed in photos: gas stovetop + oven, decent counter run, microwave, fridge — rare certainty in this cluster
  • ~₪2,850/mo all-in (₪2,000 + ~₪240 arnona + bills) — big budget headroom for co-working or a car
  • Groceries as good as Tiberias gets: Yesh Hesed and the Big center supermarkets within a short walk — vegan/GF-friendly stocking
  • Bus stop with many lines nearby (ad) for the hop to the CBS and the hourly 836 to Tel Aviv
  • 3 rooms — a closable second room for their desk (a desk+chair setup is even pictured, though possibly the tenant's)
  • Private landlord (no agency fee); new noise-insulated doors per ad
–
  • Faces HaNasi Weizmann — the main upper-Tiberias road; street noise is a real risk despite the insulated doors
  • Furniture is explicitly partial: two SINGLE beds + wardrobe + table/chairs — no couple's double bed, and the sofa/desk in photos may not stay
  • Condition is mixed: partial renovation, dated wardrobe, visible patched wall damage in the WC — livable but not 'inviting'
  • Tel Aviv still 2.5h+ door-to-door (local bus + 836); no train
  • Tiberias September heat (-200m); AC present but bills will show it
  • Short-term status unknown — standard rental needing landlord agreement
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability with the landlord (not stated)
  • Ask exactly which furniture stays — specifically a double bed (or joining the two singles) and a sofa
  • Ask which side the bedroom faces (courtyard vs HaNasi road) and visit at rush hour for noise
  • Verify the oven works and see the kitchen counter in person
  • Confirm arnona amount and typical bills (nothing stated)
54
Freshly-renovated Hadar studio, very cheap and clean — but a single hotplate and one room for everything MAYBE
Haifa · הדר עליון · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 28.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,000/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪150 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a clean white kitchenette with a modest counter run, sink, microwave and a full-size fridge, plus (per the listing) a portable electric hotplate — no oven and no built-in cooktop. There's some real prep space, so light cooking works, but a single hotplate is marginal for a couple cooking most meals (they'd want a second/portable burner). The plus side: it's genuinely freshly renovated, bright and spotless (gray marble-look tile, new bathroom with shower + washing machine), furnished with a double bed, wardrobe and dining table, and only ₪2,200. The catch is it's a 28 m² studio — sleeping, cooking and two laptops all share one room.
+
  • Very cheap (₪2,200) and genuinely renovated — clean, bright, move-in ready
  • Real (if modest) kitchen counter + full fridge + microwave + hotplate
  • Central Hadar Elyon: Metronit-served, walkable shops and Talpiot market nearby
  • New bathroom with shower and in-unit washing machine
–
  • Single portable hotplate, no oven, no built-in cooktop — limited for cooking most meals
  • 28 m² studio: one room for sleep + kitchen + two remote workers is tight
  • Ground floor with window bars — check light, privacy and quiet
  • Photo set mixes in other apartments
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let (listing is annual, immediate entry)
  • Ask whether a second burner / small oven can be added (or negotiate one in)
  • Check ground-floor noise/privacy and how the single room works for two laptops
  • Confirm the renovated studio shown is the actual unit
54
Renovated micro-studio steps from Rabin Square, months-long sublet, ~₪4,200 all-in — tiny, semi-basement light, compact kitchen MAYBE
Tel Aviv · הצפון הישן - צפון · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 23 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,000/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a · המחיר כולל אינטרנט, ועד בית וחשבון מים (Yad2 ארנונה לחודשיים: 1 — likely a data quirk, verify) · missing arnona not stated
very small
23sqm studio sublet at Rabbi Moshe Ben Ezra 10, a genuinely quiet side street in the Old North next to Rabin Square. Freshly renovated, clean, with a real work desk + office chair; price ₪4,000 including internet, va'ad and water, arnona listed as ~zero, so all-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250 (only electricity on top). Kitchen is compact-but-cookable: electric cooktop, small oven, fridge, microwave — though prep counter looks minimal (a dark pass-through bar is the main surface). Ground floor with high frosted windows and bars = limited natural light, and 23sqm is very tight for two people working from home.
+
  • Unbeatable everyday location: quiet street off Rabin Square — supermarkets, cafes, Ibn Gvirol/Dizengoff bus corridors all on foot
  • Sublet 'possible for a few months' — clean short-term fit, enter with a suitcase
  • ₪4,000 includes internet + va'ad + water; arnona stated ≈0 → all-in ≈ ₪4,150-4,250, under cap
  • Renovated and clean; includes a proper desk + office chair (one ready workspace)
  • Cooking possible: electric cooktop + small oven + fridge + microwave
–
  • 23sqm for a couple + two remote jobs = very cramped; only one workspace, little room for their own desk
  • Ground floor ('קומה 0') with high frosted windows + bars — semi-basement feel, limited daylight for people home all day
  • Kitchen prep space is minimal — cookable but frustrating for daily vegan+GF cooking
  • No light-rail nearby; Savidor/Arlosoroff train + Red Line ~1.7km (buses excellent though)
?
  • Confirm Sept-Nov ~2-month window (ad says 'a few months' — align dates)
  • Arnona listed as '₪1 per 2 months' — almost certainly a data quirk; verify who pays arnona and how much
  • Ask for more photos/video of the kitchen counter + daylight at midday (windows look high and frosted)
  • Would the owner allow adding their own desk (space check) and is there a second sittable work spot?
  • Boxes of new appliances in photos — confirm everything (kettle, toaster) is actually set up
54

Furnished garden unit on quiet Kibbutz Parod — rural car-country, and the photos are from 2022 MAYBE
Parod · פרוד · ₪2,750/mo rent · 2 rooms · 150 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,400/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,750 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Tidy 2-room unit in Parod's expansion with a large landscaped yard and deck, furnished (sofa, TV armchair, wardrobe, fridge, writing desk) with AC and parking. One-wall kitchen with a modest counter and what looks like a freestanding stove — cookable but not generous. Rural quiet is superb and the ad's 'long-term possible' wording implies short-term flexibility, but transit is the weak point: bus 361 from the Parod junction to Karmiel then train, ~3h door-to-door to Tel Aviv, and without a car groceries are a project. All photos are 2022 phone screenshots — current condition unverified.
+
  • Very quiet rural setting with a big private garden and deck
  • Furnished including a writing desk; AC, parking, available Aug 1 — fits Sept start
  • Short-term friendly: 'long-term possible' phrasing implies monthly flexibility
  • Intercity bus 361 (Haifa–Karmiel–Safed) stops at the Parod junction on Rt. 866
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,350 (est. arnona+bills) — inside budget
–
  • Car country: ~3h to Tel Aviv by bus+train — hard for Roy's weekly commute; headroom doesn't fund a rental car
  • No walkable supermarket (kibbutz minimarket at best) for daily vegan/GF cooking
  • One-wall kitchen with limited counter; oven not confirmed
  • Listing photos are 2022 screenshots — real current state unknown
  • Upper Galilee: 2026 Israel–Hezbollah war saw Galilee-wide sirens; June 26 ceasefire framework is fragile
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability explicitly
  • Ask for CURRENT photos — the ad reuses 2022 phone screenshots
  • Confirm oven/cooking setup and the actual unit size (150 sqm field is implausible; likely includes the yard)
  • Ask how bills are charged (garden units are often flat-rate) and whether arnona is included
  • Check walk time from the unit to the 361 stop at Parod junction
  • Re-verify the northern security situation closer to September
53

Kiryat Ata · מרכז · ₪3,000/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,650/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,000 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a compact kitchenette: a two-burner electric cooktop, a microwave and a small fridge, with minimal counter and no oven — usable for simple cooking but tight for a couple who cook most meals and want real prep space. On the plus side it is brand-new, clean and fully furnished (double bed, sofas, washing machine, dining table), a bright open studio with a pleasant open view, at a reasonable ₪3,000 including arnona and water. It is only 40 m² / 2 rooms, though, so two people on laptops share one small table, and it sits on Zevulun in the busier town center. Some photos show other units, so confirm the actual kitchenette is what is on offer.
+
  • Brand-new, clean and fully furnished, move-in ready (double bed, sofas, washing machine, microwave, dining table)
  • ₪3,000 including arnona and water
  • Central location (Zevulun 39) — walkable to shops in the town center
  • Bright open-plan studio with a pleasant open view
–
  • Kitchenette only — electric 2-burner, no oven, very little counter/prep space (weak for the couple's #1 priority)
  • Small 40 m² / 2-room studio — cramped shared workspace for two laptops
  • Town-center street (Zevulun) is likely busier and less quiet
  • Short-term availability unknown
  • Listing mixes in photos of other units (an L-shaped empty kitchen, a staged designer bedroom, an occupied gas-stove kitchen) that are not this studio
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability
  • Confirm the real kitchen is the electric 2-burner kitchenette (no oven) and whether that is enough for their cooking
  • Inspect actual counter/prep space in person
  • Assess whether two people can comfortably work on laptops in 40 m²
  • Ask about street noise on Zevulun in the center

Polished all-inclusive Ben Yehuda studio, monthly-basis-only at ₪3,650 — but a microwave-only kitchenette with no cooktop or oven MAYBE
Jerusalem · מרכז העיר · ₪3,650/mo rent · 1 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
₪3,650/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-termvery small
Hotel-style renovated 20 sqm studio on the Ben Yehuda pedestrian mall (city center, West Jerusalem), 4th floor with elevator, rented on a monthly basis only with everything included — arnona, electricity, water, wifi, cable and va'ad — so all-in is ₪3,650, ~₪850/mo under the cap, and the structure fits a Sept–Nov stay perfectly. The blocker is the kitchen: a small niche with sink, microwave, kettle and coffee machine, no stovetop or oven, and the modest counter is mostly occupied by appliances — marginal-to-failing for a couple cooking vegan/GF meals daily for two months. Location is superb for Roy's commute (Jaffa Center light rail ~2 min walk, ~3 stops to Yitzhak Navon, 35-40 min trains to Tel Aviv every 30 min) and Mahane Yehuda shuk is ~10 min on foot; evening busker/nightlife noise from the midrachov is the other open question.
+
  • ₪3,650 truly all-in (arnona, electricity, water, internet, cable, va'ad stated included) — well under the ₪4,500 cap
  • "Monthly basis only" — exactly the short-term structure they need
  • New condition, tasteful full furnishing (double bed, sofa, bar table + stools, TV), modern bathroom, AC, elevator
  • Prime transit: light rail on Jaffa Rd ~2 min walk, ~3 stops to Navon station; 35-40 min trains to TLV every 30 min
  • Mahane Yehuda shuk ~10 min walk plus city-center supermarkets — strong vegan/GF shopping
–
  • Kitchen: microwave-only kitchenette nook — no stovetop, no oven, small appliance-crowded counter; daily cooking for two would be a struggle
  • 20 sqm for two remote workers: a bar table and a small round table, no room for a proper second workstation or their desk
  • Ben Yehuda midrachov below — buskers and nightlife into the night, especially weekends; floor 4 only partly helps
  • Fridge not visible in photos — likely small/under-counter
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month Sept–Nov window is actually available (monthly-only is stated, the dates aren't)
  • Ask whether a portable induction hob may be added and how much clear counter + fridge capacity there really is — this decides the listing
  • Ask which way the windows face and how loud the midrachov gets at night
  • Confirm the all-inclusive price holds unchanged for a 2-month autumn stay
52
Freshly renovated 4-room in Shikun Bet at ~₪3,200 all-in, walkable to the CBS — but the sparkling kitchen shows no cooktop or oven and the furnishing is workers'-flat sparse MAYBE
Tiberias · שיכון ב · ₪2,300/mo rent · 4 rooms · 80 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,950/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,300 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The renovation is real: new marble-look floors, new white kitchen cabinetry with a good counter run and fridge, AC, and it's ~10 min on foot from Tiberias CBS (hourly 836 to Tel Aviv). But the photos show zero cooking appliances — no hob, no oven, just sink and fridge — and the furnishing pattern (double bed + rooms of single beds, 'suitable for partners') reads as basic worker/partner housing: beds on plain frames, one sofa, a dining table, nothing cozy. For a couple whose #1 criterion is a cookable kitchen, this is a verify-first listing; if a cooktop/oven exist (or the landlord adds them), the price, space and CBS walkability make it a reasonable value play, with the third bedroom as a closable office.
+
  • Genuinely renovated and clean — new floors, new kitchen units, bright rooms
  • ~₪3,215/mo all-in (₪2,300 + ~₪295 arnona + bills) — comfortable headroom
  • ~10 min walk to Tiberias CBS → hourly direct 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m); buses + makolet by the building per ad
  • 4 rooms / 80 sqm: dining table for two laptops plus a spare closable room for their desk
  • Kitchen counter/prep space is decent IF appliances are added; ad claims a quiet street
–
  • No cooktop or oven visible anywhere in the kitchen photos — as shown, it fails the couple's non-negotiable unless equipped
  • Sparse, institutional furnishing (bare beds, minimal living gear) — functional, not the warm 'place to BE' they want
  • Derech HaGvura is a through-road for the neighborhood — 'quiet street' claim needs an on-site check
  • Agency ad aimed at partners/workers; short-term status unknown
  • Tel Aviv 2.5h+ door-to-door; Tiberias Sept heat
?
  • FIRST: does the kitchen have (or will the landlord install) a cooktop and oven? Dealbreaker question
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (not stated)
  • Confirm the double-bed bedroom is included as photographed and whether a washing machine exists
  • Visit for street noise on Derech HaGvura (buses pass nearby)
  • Ask what vaad bayit, if any, applies (nothing stated)

Cookable galley kitchen, no oven; cramped studio for two SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,339/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,989/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,339 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Kitchen is genuinely cookable: a real continuous countertop with a portable 2-burner gas stovetop and a full top-freezer fridge/freezer — only an oven is missing (negotiable, they can add a countertop oven, though it would eat into the modest counter). Everything else is a budget compromise: a small single-room studio with just one narrow wall table and a single chair, sparse furniture (bare mattress), and a lively, gritty Hadar location steps from Talpiot Market — excellent for fresh vegan produce but not the quiet they want. For two people both working remotely from home all day, the single tiny work surface and cramped room are the real weakness here, more than the kitchen.
+
  • Real cookable kitchen: continuous counter + portable 2-burner gas stovetop + full fridge/freezer; only the oven is missing (and that's negotiable for them)
  • Steps from Talpiot Market (Shuk Talpiot) - fresh produce ideal for vegan home cooking, plus supermarkets and cafes walkable
  • Under budget at 4,339/mo
  • Fully available across the window (61/61 days, 1-night min) - easy to book a ~2-month stay
  • Renovated, clean bathroom; in-unit washing machine; A/C; courtyard-facing side offers some noise buffer
  • Central Haifa with frequent Metronit/buses; reasonable base for reaching Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • No oven - they'd need to buy a countertop one, which would consume scarce counter space
  • Only a small portable 2-burner cooktop and a single-run, modest counter - tight for a couple cooking most of their meals
  • Tiny single-room studio: cramped for two people who are both home working all day
  • Workspace inadequate for two: one narrow wall table and a single chair; they bring only one desk and there's no separate spot
  • Sparse, basic furnishing (bare mattress, dated budget feel)
  • Hadar by the shuk is lively and gritty, not quiet - a mismatch for their stated quiet preference
  • No one-seat ride to Tel Aviv; must get downhill to a train/bus station first
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,989/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm the host will do a ~2-month Sept-Nov stay at the monthly rate - it's an Airbnb short-term listing; availability looks fully open but the long-stay price/terms are unconfirmed
  • Ask if a second work-friendly chair/surface can be added and get room dimensions - two remote workers need two viable spots
  • Verify real street-noise levels (courtyard vs street side) given the busy market area
  • Confirm gluten-free packaged grocery options nearby (fresh produce is covered by the shuk; GF specialty items less certain)
50
Sleek Haifa studio, great transit, but tiny kitchenette & no oven SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,164/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,814/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,164 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a small but real kitchenette: sink, wood cabinets, a 2-burner electric cooktop, microwave, kettle and fridge — but NO oven and only minimal prep counter, which the host frames as 'light meals' only. For a couple cooking most of their vegan/GF food that's workable-but-tight (stovetop yes, buy a countertop oven), not comfortable. It's also an open-plan studio, so two people on video calls share one room, and the effective price rises above budget once 18% VAT for Israelis is added (~₪4,914). Upside: beautifully renovated, quiet-insulated, superbly located Downtown for a car-free couple.
+
  • Beautifully renovated modern studio: AC, fast Wi-Fi, smart TV, elevator
  • Real (if small) kitchenette with 2-burner cooktop + full-size fridge — cooking possible
  • Excellent car-free transit: ~10-15 min walk to Haifa Center-HaShmona train + Metronit
  • Groceries close (Wadi Nisnas produce, downtown supermarkets), vegan-friendly German Colony
  • Base ₪4,164 under budget; short 2-month stays accommodated
–
  • No oven; minimal prep counter — host calls it a 'light meal' kitchenette
  • Open-plan studio: two remote workers, one room, no call privacy
  • Only a fold-down bar shelf + 2 stools for workspace — cramped for two
  • 18% VAT for Israelis pushes effective rent to ~₪4,914/mo — over budget
  • Downtown/port nightlife area — quiet is a mild risk
?
  • Confirm total price incl. 18% VAT before pursuing — base rate is misleading
  • Clarify whether Israeli-citizen renters are eligible (operator asks passport + tourist visa)
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov window + monthly rate (61/61 days open, min 3 nights)
  • Ask about a second work surface given the open studio
  • Hazorea reachable by train+bus; Gedera is a long ~2h haul south

Cramped single-hob kitchenette; great market, lively area SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,434/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,084/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,434 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen — their #1 criterion — is a small, cramped kitchenette: a single portable electric hotplate, no oven, and little clear counter, though a full fridge/freezer plus a microwave sit on the adjacent enclosed balcony, and the balcony bistro table offers overflow prep space. It's a notch above a token kitchenette because cooking gear is provided, but it falls short of a comfortable cook's kitchen for two people cooking most of their meals. The big offsetting draw is location: it's right on Talpiot Market (fresh produce plus a vegan food store and bakery) — ideal for vegan+GF whole-food cooking — and it's under budget, furnished, and centrally walkable with no car needed. The catch is that the same market brings nightlife (night fair, bar, brewery), so their preference for QUIET is a real question.
+
  • Cooking gear already provided: full-size fridge/freezer + microwave (on the enclosed balcony) and a portable hotplate — nothing to buy
  • Right on Talpiot Market — fresh-produce market with a vegan food store and bakery; outstanding groceries at the doorstep for their vegan + gluten-free diet
  • Under budget at ~₪4,434/mo and fully furnished for a ~2-month stay bringing only a desk
  • Spacious, bright main room with AC; enclosed balcony with sliding glass doors gives a plausible semi-separate second workspace for two remote workers
  • Central, dense, walkable Hadar HaCarmel location — no car needed; Carmelit funicular + frequent buses reach downtown train (Haifa Center HaShmona) for Tel Aviv
–
  • Kitchen is the weak point and it's their #1 criterion: small cramped kitchenette, only a single portable hotplate, NO oven, and minimal clear counter for two people cooking most meals
  • Sits on top of Talpiot Market's nightlife (night fair, bar, brewery, food stalls) — lively and potentially noisy, at odds with their preference for QUIET
  • No dedicated second room; both work areas are within/adjacent to one main room
  • Older building; kitchen and fixtures are dated and utilitarian
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,084/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Short-term availability is listed (Airbnb, all 61 days open in the window) — but confirm the ~₪4,434 monthly rate holds for the full ~2-month stay and that specific Sept-Nov 2026 dates are open
  • Ask the host whether a second burner can be added and whether the enclosed-balcony table is usable as real prep/overflow surface
  • Check actual noise from the market/bar/brewery below, and whether the main room/balcony face the quieter interior courtyard (the balcony view suggests a back courtyard)
  • Confirm there is truly no oven; ask if a countertop oven may be added

rent ₪2,800 + arnona ₪250 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Renovated-looking 3.5-room (60sqm, floor 3) in Givat HaRakafot, Kiryat Ata at ₪2,800 + stated ₪250 arnona → all-in ≈ ₪3,400. The advertised 'stunning kitchen' is real cabinetry: a big bordeaux kitchen with generous counters and storage, fridge and washing machine — but no cooktop and no oven are visible anywhere, and the bedroom photo shows a bare room with only a built-in wardrobe (no bed), with just a recliner and coffee table elsewhere. Cleaner and brighter than its Palmach-street neighbors, but two make-or-break unknowns (cooking appliances, actual furniture) keep it at a cautious maybe.
+
  • Best kitchen WORKSPACE in the Kiryat Ata trio: long counters, huge storage, fridge + washer
  • Renovated, clean, bright finish; 3.5 rooms = easy two-workspace layout if furnished
  • Quiet location (stated) in residential Givat HaRakafot, near the neighborhood's shops/Metronit
  • All-in ≈ ₪3,400 — comfortable headroom; immediate availability; no agent fee
–
  • No stovetop AND no oven visible — worst-case it's a beautiful kitchen you can't cook in without buying both
  • Photos show no bed and almost no furniture despite the 'furnished' flag
  • ₪500/mo pricier than the equally-located Palmach 10 / HaMaccabim 10 options
  • No train in Kiryat Ata — TLV ~1h45m-2h
?
  • Confirm landlord agrees to ~2-month Sept-Nov stay (short_term unknown)
  • Ask directly: is there a gas hob/cooktop and an oven? (nothing visible in photos)
  • What furniture is actually included — is there a bed, sofa, dining table?
  • If unfurnished in practice, drop it — 2-month stays can't absorb furnishing

Quiet Tel Baruch North cottage unit, 2.5 rooms + safe room, ₪4,000 incl. arnona+water — dated interior and the kitchen is an unseen 'kitchenette' SKIP
Tel Aviv · תל ברוך צפון · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,650/mo all-inincl. arnona · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + est. utilities ₪650 · 4000 שח לחודש כולל מיסים ומים
confirm short-term
50sqm 2.5-room unit of a cottage at Yokheved Bat Miriam 34, Tel Baruch North — a calm, green residential neighborhood in north TLV. ₪4,000 including arnona and water (electricity on top → ≈₪4,200-4,300 all-in), furnished, immediate entry, aimed at 'student or couple', includes a mamad. Only 2 photos exist and both show dated 80s-style furniture; the ad itself says 'מטבחון' (kitchenette) and there is no kitchen photo — so the #1 criterion is an open risk. Space-wise 2.5 rooms works well for two workspaces.
+
  • ₪4,000 including arnona + water → ≈₪4,200-4,300 all-in, under cap
  • 2.5 rooms / 50sqm — room for two separate work spots + their own desk
  • Very quiet residential street; private-house neighborhood
  • Mamad (safe room) in the unit; washing machine, fridge, AC, vacuum/iron included
  • 'Suits a couple', immediate entry — landlord already expects flexible tenants
–
  • Kitchen described only as 'מטבחון' and never photographed — high risk it fails their cooking bar (oven unlikely)
  • Visible furniture is dated (grandma-style cabinets, old beds) — livable but not the bright/inviting brief
  • Ground floor of a cottage — light and privacy depend on the garden setup, unverified
  • Bus-dependent: no train/light-rail near (Bnei Brak rail ~2.3km); buses via Ramat HaHayal/Atidim
  • Only 2 photos — bathroom, living space largely unseen
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,650/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short_term 'likely', not stated)
  • Ask for kitchen photos/video first: oven? cooktop? counter length? fridge size? — decisive
  • Bed setup: photos show two narrow beds — is there a proper double?
  • Electricity billing arrangement (separate meter for the unit or flat fee?)
  • Internet included/available?

  • Private garden w/ deck+pergola — quiet, inviting outdoor space
  • Renovated 'like new', furnished, AC; fridge/stove/washer listed
  • ₪2,500 leaves large headroom
–

  • 50sqm 2-room — second workspace could be the garden in Sept-Oct weather
  • Agency listing; garden flats can be dim — light unverified
?

  • Verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)

Rehovot Marmorek — all-inclusive ground-floor flat with a work desk, but a weak galley kitchen MAYBE
Rehovot · מרמורק · ₪4,100/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 40.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
₪4,100/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
KITCHEN is a narrow galley: white cabinets, a white counter, a sink and a fridge, but no oven or stovetop visible (at most a possible portable single hob) and only modest prep space — marginal for a couple who cooks most meals. The unit is lived-in and somewhat worn/cluttered, but it does offer a real desk with a monitor and chair (a proper workspace), a renovated bathroom with a rain shower, and everything bundled into the rent (electricity, water, taxes, cable, internet). Rehovot's location near Gedera is the main draw.
+
  • All-inclusive ₪4,100 (electricity, water, taxes, cable and internet) — predictable cost
  • Real work setup present (desk + chair), plus a renovated rain-shower bathroom
  • Rehovot: train to Tel Aviv ~30 min, near Weizmann, ~15 min to the Gedera family
  • Ground floor — no stairs
–
  • Galley kitchen with no visible oven/stovetop and limited counter — poor fit for the couple's top priority
  • Lived-in, worn and cluttered condition; open clothing rack rather than a proper wardrobe
  • Ground floor can mean less quiet/privacy
  • ₪4,100 is mid-range for what is a small, dated-feeling 40m² unit
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month (Sept–Nov 2026) short-term let is acceptable — listing is annual-oriented
  • Ask specifically about cooking appliances: is there a stovetop and/or oven, or only a sink + fridge?
  • Clarify whether the unit will be cleared/cleaned and what furniture stays for a short-term tenant
  • Ask about noise and privacy on the ground floor

46
Clean studio, but 1-burner kitchenette, no oven, small counter SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,447/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,097/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,447 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is clean but modest: a real gray counter with base cabinets/drawers, a full-size fridge/freezer and a proper sink, but NO oven and only a single portable electric hotplate (one burner) plus a microwave — thin for a vegan+GF couple who cook most meals, and prep space shrinks once the sink is accounted for. It is a freshly renovated, furnished studio (bed, sofa-chair, wheeled table, wardrobe, folding dining table) with a standout private wooden terrace overlooking Haifa bay, priced just under budget at 4,447. The appliance gaps are 'negotiable' by the couple's own rule — they can add a countertop oven / 2-burner hob — but the small counter would get crowded, and the single room gives no acoustic separation for two simultaneous video calls. It sits up the Carmel slope on Wingate Blvd: a mini-market is next door, but reaching Tel Aviv means a local bus/Metronit down to the train (~2h), a real friction with no car, plus a long outdoor staircase to the unit.
+
  • Freshly renovated, clean and bright; new tiling and fixtures throughout
  • Standout private wooden terrace with panoramic Haifa bay/port view (rare plus)
  • A genuine if small kitchen — full-size fridge/freezer, real inset sink, base cabinets + a bank of drawers (not a hotplate-on-minifridge)
  • Furnished (bed, blue sofa-chair, wheeled table, wardrobe, folding dining table + 2 chairs) — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Price 4,447 just under the 4,500 budget
  • Mini-market next door at Wingate 6; fuller supermarkets ~0.7 km in Hadar
  • Full window available (61/61 days) and monthly stays allowed
  • Mild Sept-Nov Haifa weather makes the terrace usable as an outdoor work/call spot
–
  • No oven and only a single portable electric hotplate (one burner) — weak for a couple cooking most meals vegan + gluten-free (lots of from-scratch cooking, grains, legumes, roasting)
  • Counter/prep space is only modest and the sink eats into it; adding a countertop oven plus a bigger hob would crowd the limited surface
  • Studio layout: one room for two simultaneous remote video calls, no second workspace or acoustic separation
  • Up on the Carmel slope — intercity buses and trains to Tel Aviv are down at sea level (~2.5 km); TLV trip is ~2h via a local-bus/Metronit + train transfer, a real friction with no car
  • Access is via a long outdoor wooden staircase — awkward hauling groceries without a car
  • Wingate is a through-boulevard with a local reputation for traffic noise/honking (this set-back unit may be insulated, but worth verifying)
  • Vegan/GF specialty items likely need a trip to a larger store in Hadar or Carmel Center
  • Over budget: ≈₪5,097/mo all-in (bills estimated at ₪650 — ad states none)
?
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov dates and that the host accepts a ~2-month monthly booking at this rate (short-term availability listed as open but unconfirmed for the specific stay)
  • Verify the specific unit's quietness given Wingate's traffic-noise reputation — check it is set back from the road
  • Ask whether the host will add/allow a countertop oven and a 2-burner hob, and whether counter space fits both
  • Check how many steps the outdoor staircase has — relevant for a 2-month stay with no car and grocery runs
  • Confirm frequency of buses 76/132/133 and Metronit 5 down to the train for Tel Aviv, and connections to family in Kibbutz Hazorea and Gedera

45
Nice studio, great transit — but no stovetop AND no oven MAYBE
Haifa · ₪3,745/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,395/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,745 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen, their #1 criterion, is the weak point: a real single-run granite counter with a full-size fridge/freezer, microwave, kettle and Nespresso — but NO stovetop and NO oven anywhere, only a microwave. The couple said missing ONE appliance is negotiable, but here BOTH are absent, so they'd have to buy a portable hob AND a countertop oven, and prep space is only modest single-run counter (no dedicated second surface). It's clean, fully furnished, well under budget, and the Lower City location is superb for a car-free couple (walk to HaShmona train + Metronit BRT). Downsides: it's essentially a studio (tight for two on calls) on a lively, not-quiet downtown street.
+
  • Full-size fridge/freezer (top-freezer) plus a real granite counter run — genuine prep surface, not a mini-fridge kitchenette
  • Excellent car-free transport: walkable to Haifa Center–HaShmona train (Tel Aviv ~1hr, 4 trains/hr peak) and Metronit BRT lines 1 & 2
  • Well under budget at ₪3,745/mo (cap ₪4,500)
  • Clean, bright, freshly renovated and fully furnished — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Downtown Lower City has walkable groceries plus vegan spots and GF bakeries
–
  • No stovetop AND no oven — both primary cooking instruments missing; only a microwave. Would need to buy a portable hob and a countertop oven
  • Counter is a single modest run largely taken up by microwave + appliances + sink; no dedicated second prep surface
  • Effectively a studio: bed, dining and kitchen in one room — hard for two remote workers to take calls simultaneously
  • Lower City downtown is lively with bars/nightlife — a real concern for a couple that values quiet
  • Only one small dining table as a work surface beyond the desk they bring
?
  • Confirm ~2-month availability within Sept–Nov 2026 (availability_window not listed; it's an Airbnb monthly stay)
  • Ask the host whether a stovetop/hob and/or a small oven can be provided — that would flip the kitchen verdict
  • Verify evening/weekend street noise on Sderot HaMaginim before committing (quiet is a stated priority)
  • Clarify whether it is truly a single studio room or has any partition for a second workspace

3-room yechidat diur (floor 0) in Rishon's prestigious, quiet Nobel-laureates quarter. Photos show near-new finishes, a bedroom with a real desk + office chair, and two modern bathrooms — but small, high windows throughout give a dim semi-basement feel, and the 'kitchen' is a single ~2m counter with sink and fridge: no visible cooktop, no oven, no upper cabinets. ₪4,500 includes arnona + cables, so electricity/water push all-in to ~₪4,800+, over the cap. For a couple who cook everything and will be home a lot, the kitchen and light are the wrong compromises at a max-budget price.
+
  • Quiet, upscale west-Rishon neighborhood; parking included
  • Price includes arnona + cables (₪4,500) — predictable costs
  • Real workspace: desk + office chair already in place; 2 bathrooms
  • New/renovated condition, immediate entry, washing machine
–
  • Kitchen likely fails their #1 need: no cooktop or oven visible, no upper cabinets — counter-only kitchenette
  • Small high windows, floor 0 — dim, semi-basement feel; they'll be inside a lot
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,800+ once electricity/water added — over the ₪4,500 cap
  • Standard rental, short-term not stated — needs landlord agreement
  • Train not walkable (~2.6km to Moshe Dayan); buses good (College of Management corridor) but car-lite living is bus-dependent
?

All bills included at ₪1,800 ('כולל כל החשבונות') = the cheapest all-in of the cluster with ~₪2.7k headroom. Transit is a real strength: Kiryat Motzkin station (inter-city coastal line, ~36 direct southbound trains/day) is ~700m ≈ 10 min walk. But for a couple cooking most vegan+GF meals, this kitchen most likely fails on viewing; the big table would have to double as prep space. Photos are NOT the same unit as HaShomer 30 (different rooms/kitchens) — the dup flag is same-template noise.

Photos show a fresh white renovation: black sofa and office chair mid-staging, double mattress on a platform, wet-room shower, and a neat kitchen counter with sink and washing machine — but no fridge, hob or oven visible anywhere. Neve Yosef is a weak, bus-dependent neighborhood; for two indoor-working cooks the basement-level light and unverified kitchen are the deal risks.

Netanya (Amat Poleg) semi-basement unit — weak kitchenette, top of budget SKIP
Netanya · אמת פולג · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2.0 rooms · 50.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a compact kitchenette that undercuts this couple's #1 priority: a short black counter mostly taken up by the sink, a small countertop toaster-oven, white cabinets and a washing machine under-counter — but no visible stovetop/hob and no built-in oven, and little real prep surface. It presents as a renovated semi-basement/garden-level dwelling unit (drop ceiling, fluorescent tube, small window wells) so natural light is limited, though there is a small outdoor patio. It is otherwise tidy and fully furnished with a real bedroom and a long wooden bar that would work well for two laptops, but at 4,500 it is the top of budget for a modest kitchen.
+
  • Fully furnished and move-in ready; renovated laminate floors, AC, private (uncovered) parking, immediate entry
  • Long wooden bar doubles as a good two-laptop workspace; real separate bedroom
  • Small private patio with greenery; washing machine and full-size fridge included
  • Netanya, coastal; train to Tel Aviv (~30 min)
–
  • Kitchenette fails the core need: sink + countertop toaster-oven only, no visible stovetop or oven, minimal prep counter — poor for cooking most meals
  • Reads as a semi-basement/ground dwelling unit — drop ceiling, fluorescent lighting, small window wells = limited daylight
  • 4,500/mo — top of budget for a small (50 m²) modest unit
  • Amat Poleg is a car-oriented residential pocket in south Netanya — grocery/errands likely need a bus or a walk; far from family in Gedera and Hazorea
?
  • CONFIRM ~2-month short-term availability — listed as annual (12 payments), screened as annual-only
  • If cooking matters, ask whether a cooktop/hob (even a portable induction) and oven can be added — as shown it is a kitchenette, not a cook's kitchen
  • Verify how much is below grade and how much natural light the unit actually gets; check bathroom (some mold/wear visible)
  • Photos 09–11 (grey-tile kitchens/room) are the 'similar ads' carousel, not this unit — real unit is photos 00–08

Quiet Tzahala 2-rm sublet with real oven+stove and a work nook — but pre-TAMA with only 30 days' eviction notice, and no bed SKIP
Tel Aviv · גני צהלה, רמות צהלה · ₪3,900/mo rent · 2 rooms · 48 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
48sqm 2-room + hall ('הול לאזור עבודה אישי') at Ir Shemesh 43, Ramot Tzahala/Neve Sharett — leafy, genuinely quiet garden surroundings, masses of parking, 'very low arnona'. Kitchen is old but real: freestanding oven+4-burner stove, fridge, microwave — small counter though. The dealbreaker risk is explicit in the ad: the building goes to TAMA 'very soon' and the tenant gets ONE MONTH'S notice before eviction — a mid-stay forced move during a planned Sept-Nov stay is a live possibility. Bedroom has a closet but NO bed, and the couple owns no bed. All-in ≈ ₪4,400-4,550 (3,900 + low arnona + ~₪450 bills) — at the cap.
+
  • Real oven + stovetop (photo-verified) — cookable, if tight on counter
  • Dedicated hall/work-nook advertised for a personal work area + separate living room = two workspaces feasible
  • Very quiet green pocket; garden view; abundant parking (+ lot 50m away)
  • 'Very low arnona' per ad; pets allowed; balcony
  • Owner explicitly renting short/flexibly because of the TAMA timeline — no landlord-agreement friction
–
  • Eviction with 30 days' notice once TAMA starts ('בקרוב מאוד') — real risk of a forced move mid-stay; planning poison for a fixed Sept-Nov window
  • No bed in the bedroom — they'd have to buy bed + mattress for 2 months
  • Dated and tired: worn sofa, old bathroom, small kitchen counter; not the bright-inviting brief
  • Weak transit corner of TLV: no train/light-rail near, bus-dependent (~30+ min to center)
  • All-in ≈ ₪4,400-4,550 — little to no headroom under cap once furniture purchase is added
  • Over budget: ≈₪4,550/mo all-in exceeds the ₪4,500 cap (auto-downgraded to skip)
?
  • The decisive question: what is the actual TAMA permit/start status? Can the owner commit in writing that Sept-Nov won't be interrupted (or offer a discount/exit clause)?
  • Confirm exact all-in: arnona amount, va'ad, typical electricity
  • Will the owner provide a bed/mattress for the stay?
  • Check bus lines from Devora HaNevia/Ramat HaHayal to central TLV for Roy's weekday pattern
  • Washing machine present? (not listed)

confirm short-term
The kitchen is a small stone counter with base cabinets, a portable 2-burner gas stove and a little toaster oven plus fridge (photos 00, 03) — so more cooking capacity than a token kitchenette, but the prep space is very limited and the whole kitchen looks tired and dim. Condition overall is shabby (thin mattress on a metal frame, grimy window frame, cramped shower). It's 25 m², one room, at just ₪1,500. The listing's photo montage mixes in much nicer apartments that are not this unit — the real studio is the modest one.
+
  • Extremely cheap at ₪1,500 (12 payments, no listed arnona)
  • Has a portable 2-burner cooktop plus a small toaster oven — basic cooking is possible

Cheap but weakest kitchen — no oven, only partial furniture; skip for a cooking couple SKIP
Nahariya · ₪2,500/mo rent · 3.0 rooms · 70.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪3,450/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona ₪300 + est. utilities ₪650
border areaconfirm short-term
The kitchen is the weak point, and it is this couple's #1 priority: a small galley with white/wood cabinets, a black stone counter, sink and fridge, but no oven (gas cooktop only) and little prep space — thin for a couple who cooks most meals and bakes gluten-free. The unit is also only PARTIALLY furnished (AC, fridge, cooktop, closets — no sofa/bed/table shown), so it is not turn-key for a 2-month furnished stay. It is a cheap (2,500) older 3-room / 70 m2 on the top floor with terrazzo floors and only public parking, in the same Nahariya border-risk area.
+
  • Cheap at 2,500/mo, under budget
  • Bright rooms with greenery, 3-room / 70 m2
  • Elevator in the building; central, walkable town
–
  • No oven and a small galley kitchen with little counter — fails the couple's top priority of a good kitchen
  • Only partial furniture (no sofa/bed/table listed) — not turn-key for a short furnished stay
  • Top floor (4 of 4), older terrazzo finish
  • Only public parking (shared lot), no private spot
  • Ad padded with the same shared 'promo gallery' photos as the other two Nahariya ads, so several images are not this unit
?
  • Confirm whether a ~2-month SHORT-TERM let is possible (annual listing, 12 payments/yr) AND exactly what furniture/appliances — especially an oven — would be provided
  • Border/siren caveat: Nahariya is near the Lebanon border with rocket sirens and impacts in 2026 — verify current security for Sept-Nov and comfort with siren risk before pursuing

Immaculate 16m2 center studio, but only a minimal kitchenette and likely annual lease MAYBE
Jerusalem · מרכז העיר · ₪3,750/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 16.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
≥₪3,750/mo all-inincl. utilities
rent ₪3,750 + arnona n/a · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
Cooking most meals for two here would be a real stretch: the kitchen is a single short run of counter with a sink, a mini-fridge tucked underneath, an electric cooktop and one small stretch of prep space - no oven and no second surface. It's a clean, functional kitchenette rather than a cook's kitchen, and for a vegan/GF couple doing most meals it would feel cramped fast. Everything else is genuinely lovely: fully renovated, bright and spotless, with a brand-new bathroom and AC, and it sits as a quiet rear ground-floor unit bordering Independence Park just off King George in the city center - central yet reportedly quiet. But at 16m2 it's the smallest option, furnishing is only partial, there's no real desk for two laptops, and the terms point to a 12-month lease rather than a 2-month stay.
+
  • Fully renovated, bright, spotless; new bathroom and new AC
  • Quiet rear/garden position bordering Independence Park despite central location
  • Very central - walkable to shuk, light rail and Navon train station (~35 min to Tel Aviv)
  • Cheapest option at ₪3,750
–
  • Minimal kitchenette: short counter, cooktop + mini-fridge, no oven, little prep space - poor for a couple who cook most meals
  • Smallest unit at 16m2 - very tight for two who cook and both work from home
  • Screening flags annual-only; description shows 12 payments/year (long-lease signal)
  • Only partially furnished; no proper desk/workspace for two laptops
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability - this looks like a 12-month annual lease, which likely disqualifies it
  • Confirm exactly what furniture is included (listed as partial)
  • Ask about real usable counter space and whether any oven can be added
  • Verify the 'quiet rear' claim against King George street noise

Beautiful Hadar Elyon vacation flat with sea view — but rent alone eats the whole all-in budget and the kitchen may be SHARED SKIP
Haifa · הדר עליון · ₪4,400/mo rent · 2 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,050/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,400 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Styled, genuinely inviting 45sqm vacation apartment on Golomb Blvd (Hadar Elyon): bright living room with bay view, separate bedroom, designer bathroom, smart TV, linen/cleaning service, rentable by day/week/month — short-term native, Carmelit Golomb station ~2 min away. Two killers: (1) none of the 7 photos shows the kitchen, and the ad text 'מטבח משוטף בין עוד יחידה פשוטה יותר' reads as a kitchen shared with another unit, equipped only with coffee machine/kettle/hob/microwave — no oven; (2) ₪4,400/mo is the entire ₪4,500 all-in ceiling before bills (if bills aren't included it busts the budget outright).
+
  • Short-term native (day/week/month) — zero availability risk
  • Bright, designed, sea/bay view — the best 'nice place to BE' interior in the cluster
  • Separate bedroom → one closable workspace possible
  • Carmelit Golomb at the door → 3 stops to downtown/train; Talpiot market ~10 min walk
  • Cleaning + linen service included
–
  • ₪4,400 rent ≈ the whole all-in budget; bills inclusion not stated — likely over ₪4.5k total
  • Kitchen not photographed and wording suggests it is SHARED with a second unit — likely fails the #1 non-negotiable
  • No oven listed (only hob/microwave/coffee gear)
  • 45sqm; vacation-flat pricing rarely negotiates down to their comfort zone
  • Ad text is garbled agency boilerplate — expect upsell friction
?
  • Only if smitten: is the kitchen PRIVATE to this unit? Demand kitchen photos (counters, hob, oven)
  • Exact monthly all-in price for a 2-month Sept-Nov stay incl. electricity/water/arnona — get it in writing
  • If they offer the 'simpler unit' instead — reassess from scratch
42

Big Katzrin house sublet with a superb cook's kitchen — but ₪4,820 before utilities blows the budget, and it's the Golan SKIP
Katzrin · גמלא · ₪4,000/mo rent · 4 rooms · 232 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪5,470/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪820 + est. utilities ₪650
A genuine sublet (short-term explicitly fine) of a large, well-kept private house 2 min walk from Katzrin's commercial center: the kitchen is the best in the cluster — gas cooktop, hood, built-in wall oven, big fridge, long L-counters — plus renovated per ad, furnished rooms, yard, and an in-house shelter. But the math kills it for this couple: ₪4,000 rent + ₪820 stated arnona = ₪4,820 before electricity/water for a 232 sqm house — realistically ₪5,500+, ~₪1,000+ over their all-in ceiling, for space they don't need. Add Katzrin's near-once-daily 3h15m bus to Tel Aviv and the Golan security picture (~18-19 km from the ceasefire line; calm mid-2026 but unresolved, and Katzrin took direct rocket hits in 2024), and there's no advantage left that price was supposed to provide.
+
  • Sublet — short-term is explicitly the offer; available from Jul 22
  • Outstanding real kitchen: gas burners + hood + built-in oven + large fridge + long counters — exactly what they cook-everything couple wants
  • Whole well-kept furnished house with big yard, front porch, two bathrooms, washing machine, in-house shelter (miklat)
  • 2 min walk to Katzrin commercial center (supermarket) and country club — best grocery walkability in the cluster
  • Quiet residential street in a small town; Golan elevation cooler than Tiberias in Sept
–
  • Over budget: ₪4,000 + ₪820 stated arnona = ₪4,820/mo before electricity/water/internet on a 232 sqm house — plausibly ₪5,500+, well past the ₪4,500 all-in ceiling
  • Golan Heights, ~18-19 km from the Israel-Syria ceasefire line: mid-2026 calm with security talks ongoing but unresolved (IDF in buffer zone); Katzrin homes took direct rocket hits in 2024 — clashes with the 'not near borders' preference (capped at maybe at best)
  • Tel Aviv transport is dire: direct 843 bus runs about once a day (~04:50, ~3h15m); otherwise bus to Tiberias then 836 — 4h door-to-door; car effectively required
  • Far more house than a couple needs — they'd be paying the over-budget premium for unused rooms
  • Dated 90s interior (tidy but not the bright/inviting brief); no living-room or yard photos shown despite ad claims
?
  • Only if they fall in love with Katzrin: ask whether utilities can be capped/included and get real winter-free Sept-Nov electricity numbers
  • Confirm exact available window covers Sept-Nov (listed from Jul 22)
  • Ask to see living room and yard photos (not in the ad)
42

Shimshit garden unit — lovely and quiet but over budget all-in, car required, modest kitchen SKIP
Shimshit · ₪4,000/mo rent · 2 rooms · 165 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,983/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona ₪333 + est. utilities ₪650 · ארנונה לחודש - 333 ש"ח. צריכת חשמל ומים בנפרד (electricity and water paid separately)
car recommendedconfirm short-term
Furnished 2-room garden unit in the upscale community yishuv Shimshit, with a genuinely beautiful private ~100sqm garden (fruit trees, patio) and total quiet. But the kitchen is a single modest run (no oven visible, hob unconfirmed), and the math breaks the budget: 4,000 rent + 333 arnona + separate electricity/water lands around 4,650-4,800/mo. No real bus service — a car is effectively mandatory. ~22-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea.
+
  • Beautiful private garden with fruit trees and patio — rare quality-of-life asset for two people home all day
  • Very quiet rural-suburban setting; pets allowed; parking included
  • Furnished (sofa, TV, double bed, dining table) and AC present
  • Agency states short-term 'likely'; listed since 2025 so landlord may be flexible
  • ~20-25 min drive to Kibbutz Hazorea (family)
–
  • All-in ~4,650-4,800/mo — exceeds the 4,500 cap
  • Kitchen is a modest single counter run: no oven visible, stovetop unconfirmed — weak for a cook-most-meals couple
  • No practical public transport (sparse regional buses 310/342 at yishuv entrance); car needed, adding cost on top of an already over-budget rent
  • No second room for a closed office; workspace = dining table only
  • Agency ad — possible brokerage fee on top
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability explicitly
  • Ask whether rent is negotiable down (long-listed unit) — only way it fits budget
  • Verify stovetop/oven existence and ask for current kitchen photos
  • Ask about brokerage fee for a 2-month stay
  • Confirm what electricity/water actually run in summer (garden irrigation? AC)

Nesher (Keren Hayesod) 4.5-room 100 m², ₪4,200 — large new kitchen but delivered empty, appliances not installed SKIP
Nesher · ₪4,200/mo rent · 4.5 rooms · 100.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,200 + arnona ₪300 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
The kitchen is large and freshly renovated — an L-shaped run of light-wood cabinets with a long dark-stone counter and sink, plus central gas and a dishwasher per the ad — so prep space is excellent, BUT the photos show it with no appliances installed (a loose gas cooktop sits on the living-room floor; no oven or fridge fitted) and the whole flat is empty and unfurnished. For a 2-month furnished, move-in-ready stay where cooking is the priority, that is a real problem: you would arrive to a shell with an uninstalled kitchen and no bed or sofa. It is also the most expensive, far larger (4.5 rooms) and higher-fee (arnona ₪600/2mo) than a couple needs. Floor 1, facing a park; several photos (furnished rooms, high-rise views, a cat) are contamination from other ads.
+
  • Very large, freshly renovated kitchen: lots of cabinets + long stone counter + sink; gas + dishwasher per the ad
  • Spacious 100 m², floor 1 facing a park, two bathrooms
  • Pet-friendly with plenty of storage
–
  • Appears unfurnished and post-renovation — appliances (cooktop/oven/fridge) not installed in the photos
  • Fails the 'furnished, move-in-ready' need for a 2-month stay
  • Most expensive (₪4,200) and far bigger than needed; arnona ₪600/2mo
  • Photo set contaminated with other apartments
  • Framed as long-term for 'serious' tenants
?
  • Confirm a ~2-month short-term is even on the table (ad wants long-term)
  • Critically: confirm it comes FULLY furnished with an installed, working oven/cooktop/fridge — otherwise not viable
  • If pursued, negotiate the price down given the size is surplus for two

Private ad for a 25sqm 2-room unit at Ta'anach 36, Neve Yosef, floor minus-3 ('open to fresh air'), ₪2,200 including arnona (~₪2,550 est. all-in with electricity/water). Same building and same white-renovation spec as agency listing 58b9e788e401b032; layouts and bathrooms differ, so these look like two subdivided units, not one duplicate — worth asking, since the other is ₪2,000 with ALL bills included and 10sqm larger on paper. Photos show a tidy unit: kitchenette counter with washing machine, full-size fridge, dark sofa, bedroom with wardrobe, greenery outside the window. No hob or oven visible. For two home-working cooks, 25sqm at basement level in a weak bus-dependent neighborhood is too small a life for two months.

  • Renovated, tidy, with full-size fridge and washing machine visible; window greenery despite floor -3

–

Agency listing with no address (coords put it south of the center near the hotel area): a lived-in family flat with laundry-draped balcony over a parking lot/street by a hotel. The kitchen does have a real freestanding gas cooker (stove + oven), granite counters and a microwave, but it's cramped enough that the fridge sits in the living room, and every photo shows the current tenants' heavy clutter — impossible to know what stays or how it presents empty. At ~₪3,200 all-in it's the same money as the freshly renovated Derech HaGvura flat and ₪350/mo more than Jabotinsky 2, while being the most tired of the three. Elevator and parking are the bright spots. Nothing here wins on price, quiet or kitchen versus its in-town rivals.

–

  • Kitchen is cramped — the fridge is in the living room; prep is a single short run
  • No address in the ad — street noise unjudgeable (balcony photo suggests hotel/parking frontage; possible evening noise)
  • Same all-in cost as the renovated d20ab29a flat and more than Jabotinsky 2, with worse condition than both
  • Short-term unknown; agency ad; unclear what furniture stays

  • Marketed as clinic/office too — residential kit likely thin
  • Workspace = one small dining table in a 1.5-room studio
  • No train access — bus to Hof HaCarmel/downtown first, longest TLV commute in cluster
?
  • Only if reconsidered: which exact unit is offered and what cooking equipment exists — ask for a full kitchen photo
  • Would the operator allow adding a portable induction hob + mini oven, and is there counter/outlet capacity?
  • Confirm exact Sept-Nov 2026 availability and the all-inclusive terms in writing
