Token kitchenette: no oven, plank counter, almost no prep space SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,902/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,552/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,902 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
Kitchen is a token kitchenette and the couple's stated dealbreaker: a portable two-burner hotplate on a narrow wooden plank that's shared with the sink, with no oven anywhere and essentially no fixed prep surface — the dining table is the only real work area. It does have a full-size two-door fridge/freezer, and the hotplate is exactly the "portable stovetop" they said they'd otherwise buy, but there's nowhere near the counter space a couple cooking most of their vegan+GF food wants. The rest is decent value: ₪3,902/mo (under budget), furnished for two months with multiple beds/desks and AC/WiFi so both can work in separate rooms, private entrance and courtyard. But the unit reads rough and semi-finished (raw brick, bare concrete), the toilet opens straight onto the kitchen prep area, and it sits on busy HaGiborim St with no walkable train.
+
  • Full-size two-door fridge/freezer (real cold storage, not a mini-fridge)
  • Portable two-burner hotplate already provided — the 'portable stovetop' they said they'd otherwise have to buy
  • ₪3,902/mo, comfortably under the ₪4,500 budget
  • Well furnished for a 2-month stay: multiple beds, tables/desks, chairs, AC and WiFi — two people can work in separate rooms
  • Private separate entrance and inner courtyard/garden; fully available (61/61 days), long stays accepted
–
  • No oven anywhere — they'd have to add a countertop oven
  • Almost no fixed prep counter: just a narrow wooden plank shared with the sink. Only real work surface is the dining table. This is the couple's explicit dealbreaker (lack of cookable SPACE)
  • Toilet/bathroom opens directly onto the kitchen prep area — unappealing for people who cook most of their own food
  • Rough, semi-finished feel: exposed raw brick, bare concrete ceilings/floors, basic wet-room bathroom; some rooms dim (small frosted window)
  • On HaGiborim St, a major arterial to the Carmel Tunnels — likely traffic noise despite the 'quiet building' claim; no walkable train station
?
  • Confirm a full ~2-month booking within Sept-Nov and the effective monthly price (₪3,902 shown; availability looks open but unconfirmed for their exact dates)
  • Verify real quiet — HaGiborim is a busy road; ask whether the courtyard unit is insulated from traffic noise
  • Check bus frequency to central Haifa and connections to Tel Aviv from Halisa/Tel Amal; no walkable train (stations are down by the bay, ~2-3 km)
  • Ask the host whether an oven can be added, and whether prepping on the dining table is acceptable to them
  • Locate nearest supermarket and any vegan/GF specialty shopping — likely a trip into central Haifa
36
Cookable kitchenette but minimal prep space; cramped for two SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,648/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,298/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪3,648 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is the deciding weakness: a short granite counter with a sink, a small "Gold Line" countertop mini-oven, a likely portable hotplate resting on it, and a full-size fridge/freezer with upper/lower cabinets. So basic cooking is possible, but usable prep space is minimal — the sink and mini-oven eat most of the counter, there's no second work surface, no built-in oven or hob. For a couple who cook most of their (vegan + GF) meals and specifically need cooking SPACE, that is the stated dealbreaker. It's within budget and an Airbnb with full short-term availability, and Talpiot Market plus supermarkets in Hadar make vegan shopping easy — but the compact unit (one main room + small bedroom), shabby mid-setup condition, and no room for two remote workers to set up push this toward skip.
+
  • Full-size fridge/freezer, granite counter with sink, a countertop mini-oven and a likely portable hotplate — basic cooking is possible without buying much
  • Within budget: 3,648/mo vs 4,500 cap
  • Airbnb with 61/61-day availability and 1-night minimum — a 2-month stay is straightforward
  • Excellent fresh-produce shopping for vegan cooking: Talpiot Market (Sirkin St) and supermarkets walkable in Hadar HaCarmel
  • Low floor / minimal stairs; AC in both living room and bedroom; furnished with double bed, sofa and TV
  • Near Carmelit (Massada station) and Hadar bus network — manageable for daily errands without a car; Haifa is well placed for family in Kibbutz Hazorea
–
  • Kitchen prep space is minimal — short counter largely taken by the sink and the mini-oven, and there is no second work surface. This is exactly the couple's stated dealbreaker (lack of cookable space)
  • Only a small countertop oven and, at best, a portable hotplate — no built-in oven and no proper stovetop
  • Compact unit (one main room + a small bedroom) with no dedicated desk/workspace; very tight for TWO simultaneous remote workers when they bring only one desk
  • Condition looks shabby / mid-setup: cracked paint above the cabinets, dusty floor with debris, ladder and broom in shots; dark, worn bathroom
  • Hadar HaCarmel is a dense, busy neighborhood — quiet not guaranteed; the ground-level/low window raises light, damp and street-noise questions
  • Train station (Haifa Center) is ~1.5 km downhill, not comfortably walkable on the Carmel slope; buses/Carmelit needed for Tel Aviv trips
?
  • Confirm the black item on top of the mini-oven is a working portable hotplate/stovetop, and whether the host permits adding a portable two-burner
  • Verify the exact floor/street — whether it is semi-basement (light, damp, street noise) given the near-ground-level window
  • Two remote workers need two work spots: check whether the small rooms can fit a second work surface beyond the one desk they bring
  • Reconfirm the specific 2-month block within Sept-Nov 2026 and the monthly rate (Airbnb shows availability but confirm directly)
  • Verify quietness of the specific street in Hadar

rent ₪2,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
The kitchen is a deal-breaker for this couple: the only real photo of it shows a token kitchenette — a sink, a mini bar-fridge and a sliver of counter, with NO cooktop and NO oven (photo 04). It's marketed as a '5-star hotel-level room, bring your clothes and move in,' i.e. serviced short-stay living, not a home for people who cook most meals. The attractive bedroom shots are explicitly labeled illustrations/renders. Upscale western-Carmel location, but the kitchen alone rules it out for cooking.
+
  • Upscale western Carmel (Derech HaYam) location
  • Furnished and marketed as immediate move-in
  • Cleaner/newer bathroom than the cheaper studios
–
  • Token kitchenette: sink + mini-fridge only, NO cooktop and NO oven — cannot cook most meals here
  • Key bedroom photos are renders ('image for illustration only'), so the real interior is unclear
  • Only 25 m² studio for two people living and working
  • ₪2,900 for what is effectively hotel-style living with no cooking
?
  • Confirm the landlord will do a ~2-month short-term let
  • Ask whether a real cooktop/oven and prep counter can be added — otherwise it's a pass
  • Request actual (non-render) photos of the specific room
  • Clarify what's really included vs. the illustrative images

Bare 1.5-room PT unit with no cooking appliances, no price, no short-term info — phone-only seller SKIP
Petah Tikva · קרית דוד אלעזר · ₪100/mo rent · 1.5 rooms · 45 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪750/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪100 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
Renovated but nearly empty 45sqm 1.5-rooms on Gush Etzion St (Kiryat David Elazar, east Petah Tikva). Photos show a folded-up bed and a leaning mattress in otherwise bare rooms, and a kitchenette with an L-counter, hood and sink but NO cooktop or oven — the ad offers 'microwave possible', fridge, 2 ACs. Price is a ₪100 placeholder (unknown; arnona included per ad), short-term is unknown, and the seller answers calls only. Light rail is a 27-min walk (PT Central); it's a bus-dependent spot ~1h+ to TLV. Too many gates failed for a couple needing a real kitchen and two workspaces.
+
  • Renovated interiors (new bathrooms, tidy kitchenette cabinets); paved covered yard
  • Arnona included per the ad; quiet residential pocket, family atmosphere
  • Floor 1, immediate entry
–
  • Kitchen fails the gate: counter exists but zero cooking appliances (no stove, no oven; 'microwave possible')
  • Shown essentially unfurnished — folded bed and bare rooms; no sofa, table or desk in photos despite 'furnished' flag
  • 1.5 rooms / 45sqm — cramped for two people working remotely all day
  • Price unknown (placeholder ₪100) and short-term availability unknown
  • Light rail 27-min walk; TLV commute ~1h+ with a bus leg
  • Seller reachable by phone only — friction to even qualify the ad
?
  • REQUIRED (if called at all): confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability — completely unstated
  • Ask the actual rent and what bills are included beyond arnona
  • Is it furnished as advertised (bed assembled, table, wardrobe)? Photos say otherwise
  • Any stovetop/oven, or permission to add one?
  • Is this a rear yard unit of the family house? privacy and entrance

₪2,100 all-in near Halisa — shabby, bare, no cooking gear in the kitchenette: skip SKIP
Haifa · נווה פז · ₪2,100/mo rent · 2 rooms · 30 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
₪2,100/mo all-inall-inclusive
landlord states arnona + utilities included
confirm short-term
30sqm '2 rooms' on Shalom Shabazi 7, Neve Paz — a small 90s pocket wedged next to Halisa on Haifa's eastern slopes, bus-dependent (Lev HaMifratz hub ~2km). Bills included at ₪2,100 and there is a small work desk, but the photos undercut the 'new from contractor' claim: bare mattresses, patchy/stained walls, debris in the bathroom, and a white kitchenette with washer but no visible cooktop/oven and skimpy counter. Fails both the kitchen gate and the 'nice place to BE' test. Not the same unit as Barzilai / Khativat Karmeli 47 / HaTish'a (photo-checked — same flipper style, different apartments).
+
  • ₪2,100 all-in stated ('כולל כל החשבונות') — big theoretical headroom
  • Small work desk present; wardrobe and basic furniture in place
  • Bus lines nearby; ~2km to Lev HaMifratz rail/bus hub
–
  • Kitchen fails: no cooking appliances visible, minimal counter
  • Condition uninviting: bare mattresses, patchy walls, mid-cleaning photos
  • 'חדש מקבלן' claim contradicted by the photos
  • Neve Paz/Halisa edge — weak area for two months of home-office evenings
  • Checks required; 30sqm tight for two
?
  • Not worth chasing unless the photos predate a real refit — if contacted anyway, ask whether the unit was finished and a kitchen installed since

₪1,500 'opportunity' in upper Tiberias — the single collage photo shows a tired, cluttered flat that fails the 'nice place to be' test SKIP
Tiberias · בן גוריון · ₪1,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 70 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪2,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪1,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
Cheapest listing in the cluster by far (₪1,500, ~₪2,350 all-in with arnona and bills) with a hilltop Kinneret view from the Ben Gurion neighborhood in upper Tiberias, near the Big center's supermarkets. But the agency posted one collage photo and it does the talking: dated pink-toned bedroom, a second room used as cluttered storage, worn 90s kitchen with a small table and old cabinets, and heavy red/white sofas — 'renovated' is not what the photos show. This couple will be indoors most of the day in end-of-summer heat; a shabby, uninviting interior is exactly what they said to skip, and the rock-bottom price is the only real advantage.
+
  • Extremely cheap: ~₪2,350/mo all-in (₪1,500 + ~₪250 arnona + bills) — massive headroom that could fund a car or co-working
  • Hilltop position with a genuine Kinneret/city view
  • Ben Gurion neighborhood is a short hop from the Big Tiberias center (large supermarkets for vegan/GF staples)
  • Furnished, AC + boiler, pets allowed, immediate entry
–
  • Photos show a tired, cluttered, dated apartment — fails their 'bright, clean, inviting place to BE' requirement outright
  • Kitchen looks old and small (table squeezed in, worn cabinets); no evidence of real prep space or a modern cooktop/oven
  • Only one collage photo from the agency — can't honestly judge kitchen, furniture, or workspace
  • Upper Tiberias is ~2 km uphill from the CBS/center — local bus or car needed before the already-long 836 to Tel Aviv (~2h10m+)
  • Short-term status unknown; standard rental via agency would need landlord agreement
  • Suspiciously cheap — likely reflects the condition
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability (short-term status unknown)
  • Request current, full photos — especially the kitchen (stove? oven? counters?) — before investing any more effort
  • Ask whether the clutter/storage is the outgoing tenant's and what the flat looks like empty

Clean, cheap Motzkin Vatika studio - but essentially no kitchen SKIP
Kiryat Motzkin · מוצקין הותיקה · ₪2,200/mo rent · 1.0 rooms · 30.0 m² · homeless · added 2026-07-14
~₪2,960/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,200 + arnona ₪110 + est. utilities ₪650
confirm short-term
This fails the couple's number-one need: there is no real kitchen. The listing lists only a large fridge, a microwave and a washing machine, with no cooktop and no oven, and no kitchen or prep surface appears in any genuine photo. It is a tidy, renovated 30 m2 studio with a grey-tiled main room, a sofa-bed, a folding table and a bookshelf, rear-facing and quiet near the Metronit and shops, and it is the cheapest at 2,200 - but for two people who cook most meals and both work on laptops, a sofa-bed studio with a microwave-only kitchenette is not workable for two months.
+
  • Cheapest: 2,200/mo
  • Clean, renovated and quiet (rear-facing)
  • Near the Metronit and shopping centres
  • Furnished (sofa-bed, folding table, shelving, large fridge, microwave, washer)
–
  • No cooktop and no oven - microwave-only 'kitchen', the dealbreaker for a cooking couple
  • No visible prep / counter space at all
  • Only 30 m2 and a sofa-bed - cramped and tiring for two over two months
  • Folding table seats one - poor for two laptops
  • Only photo 00 is this unit; the rest are 'similar listings' contamination
?
  • Confirm ~2-month short-term availability (listed as annual/12 payments)
  • Only worth it if they would accept adding a portable double hob - but there is still no counter to prep on
  • Ask to see actual kitchen photos to confirm how minimal it is

Netanya C-Tower hotel suite — kitchenette, tired furniture, and a price that excludes everything SKIP
Netanya · ₪3,800/mo rent · 2 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪4,750/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,800 + arnona ₪300 + est. utilities ₪650 · המחיר לא כולל: ארנונה ודמי ניהול. חשמל מים ומיזוג
confirm short-term
Agency-run suite in C-Tower (ex-Carmel apartment-hotel, Oved Ben Ami 2, seafront city center — the JSON coords are wrong). Photos show a hotel kitchenette (one short counter, 2-burner hob, under-counter mini-fridge, no oven), dated hotel furniture and a visibly stained mattress; the high-floor window faces east over a gas station and main road. ₪3,800 explicitly excludes arnona (₪300 stated), management fees, electricity, water and AC — realistic all-in is ₪4,800-6,000+, over budget. Short-term is easy here, but it fails the kitchen gate and the budget.
+
  • Genuinely short-term-friendly (holiday-apartment operator since 2006)
  • Beachfront/promenade location in central Netanya; supermarkets and city amenities walkable
  • Elevator, AC, immediate entry; hotel-grade bathroom
–
  • Kitchenette fails their #1 need: minimal counter, 2 burners, no oven, mini-fridge — not a daily-cooking kitchen for two
  • Price excludes arnona + management fees + electricity/water/AC — all-in very likely ₪4,800-6,000, over the ₪4,500 cap
  • Worn hotel furniture; stained-looking mattress; not an inviting long-stay home
  • Unit appears to face a gas station and main road (noise risk despite height)
  • Netanya main rail station ~2.5km — bus needed; TLV commute ~50-60 min door-to-door
  • Far from both Gedera and Hazorea family
?
  • Only if pursued anyway: get the exact management fee + typical utilities to compute true all-in
  • Confirm 2-month Sept-Nov price (hotel operators often quote seasonal/nightly rates)
  • 4 possible duplicate ads exist (no photos on disk to compare) — same operator lists many units; treat as agency stock

₪3,100 incl. arnona+water new-build studio in HaTikva near HaHagana rail — but a token kitchenette and 20sqm kill it for cooking couple SKIP
Tel Aviv · התקוה, בית יעקב, נווה צה"ל · ₪3,100/mo rent · 1 rooms · 20 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
~₪3,750/mo all-inincl. arnona
rent ₪3,100 + est. utilities ₪650 · כולל ארנונה ומים (electricity not mentioned)
confirm short-termvery small
Agency multi-unit campaign (Avixis) on Etsel St, HaTikva: brand-new 20sqm 1-room units from ₪3,100/mo including arnona and water, with fridge/bed/closet/burners promised. Photos show empty just-finished units whose 'kitchen' is a single short counter run with a sink — no oven, no real prep space; that's a fail on the #1 non-negotiable ('token kitchenette with no counter = fail'). Location is transit-decent (HaHagana rail ~750m, shuk HaTikva ~400m for cheap produce) but the neighborhood is gritty and Purple Line civil works churn the area through ~Oct 2026. Cheap, but for this couple the space and kitchen don't work.
+
  • Cheapest in cluster: ₪3,100 incl. arnona + water → all-in ≈ ₪3,300 — huge headroom
  • Brand new, never lived in; AC, boiler; immediate entry
  • HaHagana railway station ~750m (~10 min walk); buses everywhere
  • Shuk HaTikva ~5 min walk — excellent cheap produce for vegan/GF cooking
  • Agency runs many units — flexible on dates/terms, likely fine with 2 months
–
  • Kitchen fails: single tiny counter with sink, no oven, no prep space (photo-verified across units) — a hotplate-and-sink setup
  • 20sqm 1-room: no realistic setup for two remote workspaces + living
  • Photos show bare units; furniture promised in text but unverified (and shared washing machine only)
  • HaTikva streets: dense, gritty, noisy market-adjacent; not the calm-inviting brief
  • Purple Line construction disruption in the neighborhood through ~Oct 2026
  • Ad phone digits obfuscated + hard-sell agency copy — expect an aggressive funnel; possible same-campaign duplicate f3c062738aa61732 (no photos to confirm)
?
  • Only revisit if they'd accept a 2-room variant (₪3,900-4,500 per ad) WITH a real kitchen — ask for kitchen photos of the 2-room units
  • Confirm which unit the photos are; ask what furniture is actually installed at entry
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov acceptance (likely yes, agency)

  • Effectively under-furnished: no sofa, no dining table, no desk in photos

Renovated Hadar basement, but no stove/oven and a tiny sink-counter SKIP
Haifa · ₪3,936/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,586/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,936 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen fails their #1 need: photos show a full-size fridge/freezer and a real sink, but NO oven and NO stovetop at all, and only a single base cabinet mostly taken up by the sink — no genuine prep space or second surface. Since a couple who cooks most meals said only ONE missing appliance is negotiable, having both absent plus minimal counter is effectively a token kitchenette. It's a renovated but by the host's own words "low ceiling and dim lighting" basement, which is poor for two people on laptops all day and only offers one small dining table to work at. The saving grace is location — Chaim Weizmann St 5 in central Hadar HaCarmel, a short walk to Talpiot Market (fresh produce and a vegan food shop) — plus it's furnished, in budget, and available short-term.
+
  • Strong fresh-food location: ~5-10 min walk to Talpiot Market (fresh produce + a vegan food shop) and Hadar shops - ideal for a vegan/GF couple cooking daily
  • Fully furnished and move-in ready (blue sofas, raised-platform double bed, wardrobes, dining table, washer + dryer); they only need to bring a desk
  • Full-size fridge/freezer and a real sink with a good pull-down faucet; renovated finishes (subway tile, wood-look floors, recessed lighting)
  • Within budget (~4,936... approx 3,936/mo), has a bomb shelter, and fully available with a 1-night minimum across the Sept-Nov window
–
  • Kitchen fails their top criterion: NO oven and NO stovetop at all, and only a tiny sink-counter with no real prep area or second surface - they'd have to buy both a hotplate and a countertop oven with nowhere to put them
  • Basement unit - by the host's own description 'low ceilings and dim lighting' - meaning poor natural light for two people working on laptops all day and taking video calls
  • Only one small dining table as a work surface; no separate second workspace for two remote workers
  • Hadar is a dense, busy neighborhood and Haifa is steeply hilly; with no car the train station is a ~1.5-2 km downhill walk (a steep climb back), so they'd lean on buses
?
  • Ask the host whether a stovetop and/or oven can be added - but the sink counter is too small to host both plus any prep, so this may not be solvable
  • Verify quietness of Weizmann St and the specific unit (a basement may buffer street noise, though Hadar itself is lively)
  • Short-term looks fine (listed 1-night min, all 61 days open in window) but re-confirm a ~2-month Sept-Nov booking and the price with the host
28

The upside is all bills included (electricity, water, arnona, internet) and a one-minute walk to the Technion's Nesher gate. Photos 05–14 are other apartments (scraper contamination) — the real unit is photos 00–04.

  • ₪2,900 is steep for a 30 m² studio; framed as annual

Pinui-binui stopgap in Yad Eliyahu: flexible months but tired, near-unfurnished, and over budget SKIP
Tel Aviv · יד אליהו · ₪4,500/mo rent · 2.5 rooms · 50 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
50sqm 2.5-room at Emek Ayalon 3 (Yad Eliyahu), rented by the owner 'for several months' ahead of pinui-binui demolition — so the short-term flexibility is genuine. Everything else is weak for this couple: photos show a tired flat that is essentially unfurnished (one bed, one worn sofa, empty rooms), and the kitchen — though it has a decent counter run — shows no stove, no oven and no fridge. At ₪4,500 + arnona + bills it lands ≈ ₪5,000-5,100 all-in, clearly over the ₪4,500 cap, for a place that would still need furniture and appliances. HaHagana rail is ~1.2km; the Ayalon highway ~250m west adds background hum.
+
  • Genuinely short-term by design (owner renting until pinui-binui) — no landlord persuasion needed
  • 2.5 rooms / 50sqm — space for two workspaces in principle
  • HaHagana train ~15 min walk; good bus corridors (La Guardia); central-ish location
  • Enclosed balcony; pets allowed; AC + boiler
–
  • All-in ≈ ₪5,000-5,100 (4,500 + ~₪200 est. arnona for Yad Eliyahu 50sqm + ~₪400 bills) — over budget; estimates, nothing stated in ad
  • Essentially unfurnished for their needs: no table, no desks, no visible fridge/washing machine; 'furnished' box not even ticked
  • Kitchen shows counters but NO cooktop/oven/fridge — would need equipping for 2 months
  • Tired pre-demolition condition throughout; grimy enclosed balcony
  • Ayalon highway ~250m — background noise likely with windows open; occasional Menora arena event crowds
  • Same one-month-notice risk profile as other pre-demolition lets — end date must be pinned down
?
  • Only worth a call if price drops sharply (owner has zero long-term alternative) — then: confirm Sept-Nov window is safe from the pinui-binui timeline in writing
  • What appliances/furniture (fridge, stove, washing machine) would the owner install?
  • Actual arnona and va'ad amounts

Kitchen is effectively absent for cooking: the furniture list and photos show a fridge and some wall/base cabinets but no cooktop and no oven, so a couple who cook most meals couldn't realistically use it — a fail on their #1 priority. It's the cheapest option (₪2,550) and does have charm as a quiet ground-floor studio with a private garden and terrace.   • Quiet ground-floor studio with a private garden and terrace
  • Furnished (fridge, AC, closet, tables, fold-out sofa); near Hof HaCarmel transit and buses
–

  • Tirat Carmel commute to Tel Aviv is ~1 hour — longest of the shortlist
  • Single room (studio) — tight for two people both working remotely
?

Har Nof 12 sqm 'vacation room' at ₪2,500 — sink-only kitchenette, dark and dated; fails the kitchen gate outright SKIP
Jerusalem · הר נוף · ₪2,500/mo rent · 1 rooms · 12 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪3,150/mo all-inrent only
rent ₪2,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-termvery small
Tiny one-room unit (12 sqm) on HaKablan St in Har Nof, a strongly Haredi neighborhood on Jerusalem's far-western edge (well inside the Green Line), marketed as a daily/weekly/monthly holiday room (₪2,500/mo). Photos show a dated, dim space: a sink + small tiled counter with a mini-fridge (מקררון) and no stovetop or oven, a cramped shower room, sofa bed plus two joined singles, and one small table with plastic chairs. No cookable kitchen, no workspace for two, and not a pleasant place to spend two hot months — the low price can't rescue it. Transit is also weak: no light rail nearby, ~20-25 min bus to Yitzhak Navon station before the 35-40 min train to Tel Aviv.
+
  • Very cheap: ₪2,500/mo with explicit monthly pricing — big budget headroom
  • Immediate entry; AC, boiler, elevator and parking listed
  • Quiet residential street in a low-traffic neighborhood
–
  • Kitchen fail: sink + small counter only, mini-fridge, no stovetop and no oven — cannot cook most meals
  • 12 sqm for two remote workers: one small table, no real workspace
  • Dark, dated, shabby finish in photos — uninviting for a long indoor stay
  • Har Nof is a strongly Haredi enclave far from the center — full Shabbat closure, awkward fit for a secular couple
  • No light rail; ~20-25 min bus to Navon station, then 35-40 min train — weakest commute of the cluster
?
  • If pursued anyway: confirm the ~2-month Sept–Nov window (monthly rate is listed but availability isn't)
  • Ask whether ₪2,500 includes arnona and utilities — not stated in the ad

Token kitchenette: 1 hotplate, no oven, no prep space SKIP
Haifa · ₪4,434/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪5,084/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,434 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a token kitchenette and the dealbreaker here: a single portable electric hotplate, a small microwave (no real oven), a mini-fridge and a small sink, with almost no prep surface — just the narrow stone slab wrapped around the sink. For a couple cooking most of their vegan + gluten-free meals, there's essentially nowhere to work and no room to even add a portable oven. The studio itself is otherwise a genuine plus: a clean, newly renovated industrial-loft space, fully furnished, within budget at ₪4,434, with good short-term availability. But it fails the #1 criterion, and it sits on HaGibborim — a busy main artery in the weaker eastern-Haifa Halisa/Tel Amal cluster — with no walkable train and only a cramped, shared open-plan work setup for two.
+
  • Fully furnished, clean, newly renovated industrial-loft studio — livable for 2 months bringing only a desk
  • Within budget at ₪4,434 (≤ ₪4,500)
  • Separate entrance with garden view; garden-facing door may buffer some street noise
  • Strong short-term signal: all 61 days open, long-stay minimum (up to 21 nights), description says 'suitable for any period'
  • Some cooking is possible if they adapt (hotplate, microwave, kettle, mini-fridge, sink)
  • Reasonable base for visiting Kibbutz Hazorea; buses on the main road link to downtown, train and Metronit
–
  • Token kitchenette fails the #1 criterion: single portable hotplate, NO oven (only a small microwave), and almost no prep counter
  • Counter is just the sink surround — no second surface and nowhere to add a portable oven for two people cooking daily
  • Weaker/lower-income eastern-Haifa neighborhood (Halisa/Tel Amal cluster), described locally as low social resilience
  • On HaGibborim, a main transport artery — likely traffic noise, working against the 'quiet' priority
  • Open-plan studio: no separation for two simultaneous video calls; only one small table as a work spot
  • No walkable train station; car-free trips to Tel Aviv mean a bus downhill plus train
?
  • Short-term availability looks good (61/61 days, 'suitable for any period') but confirm the specific ~2-month block within Sept–Nov 2026
  • Ask whether the countertop appliance is a microwave or a small oven, and whether a larger portable oven or second burner could fit given the tiny counter
  • Verify real street-noise level given HaGibborim traffic — does the garden-facing separate entrance actually insulate the unit?
  • Check walkable groceries and any vegan/GF options; specialty shopping likely requires Hadar or central Haifa

20
Aparthotel micro-studio on Menachem Begin: zero kitchen, huge junction outside — wrong product for a cooking couple SKIP
Tel Aviv · לב תל אביב, לב העיר צפון · ₪3,900/mo rent · 1 rooms · 25 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,550/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪3,900 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
25sqm 'studio apartments' operation at Menachem Begin 19 (hotel-style corridor with numbered doors, lobby plants, generic room shots — some look staged/rendered). Furnishing is hotel-room grade: bed, 55" TV, dresser, bar table, fridge, microwave — and NO cooking facilities at all: no stovetop, no oven, no kitchen counter. That's an outright fail on the #1 criterion. The window photo shows the massive Begin/Levinsky-area junction below: heavy traffic noise on one of TLV's busiest arteries. Transit is admittedly superb (Allenby Red Line ~5 min, Carlebach ~8, HaHagana rail ~15) and 'short and long periods' are welcomed, but this is a crash-pad product, not a home to cook and work in for two months.
+
  • Explicitly rents 'short and long periods' — zero availability friction
  • Outstanding transit: Red Line Allenby ~400m, Carlebach ~650m; buses everywhere; ~15 min walk to HaHagana rail
  • Elevator, entry-control, tenant laundry room, security room; renovated interiors
  • Walkable to Rothschild/Levinsky market area
–
  • No kitchen whatsoever (fridge + microwave only) — automatic fail for a couple cooking vegan+GF most meals
  • Directly over a giant multi-lane Begin junction — serious traffic noise for people home all day
  • 25sqm hotel-room layout: one bar table, no real dual-workspace potential
  • Some photos appear staged/renders — actual unit may differ; aparthotel operations often add fees
  • Old central bus station vicinity to the south is seedy at night
?
  • Skip unless everything else falls through AND they accept microwave-only eating — not their profile
  • If contacted anyway: actual unit photos, total monthly incl. bills/fees, which floor/side (courtyard-facing units would be quieter)

Micro unit near the Jerusalem light rail with a kitchenette and small fridge. Fine for one student, impossible for a cooking couple with two laptops.
+
  • Cheap (2,800) and near light rail
  • New condition; covered parking option
–
  • 15sqm, one room, kitchenette + small fridge - fails kitchen and workspace criteria outright
  • Jerusalem far from both families and TA
?
  • Confirm ~2-month Sept-Nov availability with landlord
  • Verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)
  • Size alone disqualifies - do not invest effort
15
Windowless basement studio in Rishon — nonstarter SKIP
Rishon LeZion · קרית חתני פרס נובל · ₪4,000/mo rent · 1 rooms · 46 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪4,650/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,000 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · המחיר כולל אינטרנט ויס. למעט חשמל · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
New renovated 46sqm basement studio explicitly 'without a window'. Bright is a core need; this fails outright despite decent equipment.
+
  • Well equipped (washer, dryer, TV, oven)
  • 4000 incl internet and Yes TV
–
  • Basement, explicitly no window — fails the brightness requirement
  • Poor setting for two people home all day
  • Stale June availability
?
  • Skip — no ask needed; fails a hard criterion
15
Renovated Golan house with a huge garden — beautiful, and completely off their map SKIP
Katzrin · ₪4,500/mo rent · 3 rooms · 325 m² · yad2 · added 2026-07-15
≥₪5,150/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,500 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
confirm short-term
3-room ground house in Katzrin with new furniture and a giant garden at ₪4,500. The Golan is 2.5h+ from Tel Aviv — wrong side of the country for both families and any TA access.
+
  • house + huge garden + private parking
  • new furniture, renovated
  • quiet
–
  • geography disqualifies: TA/Gedera routine access impossible, Hazorea ~1h15
  • rent at cap before bills; a big house costs more to run
  • private landlord insists on phone-only details
?
  • confirm ~2-month Sept–Nov availability with landlord
  • verify kitchen (no photos reviewed)
14
Room in a 6-way student share, kitchen not even pictured — skip SKIP
Herzliya · ₪4,157/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,807/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,157 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen — this couple's #1 criterion — is completely unassessable: not one of the 10 photos shows it, because this is a single private room in a 6-room shared apartment marketed for "students or young professionals," so the kitchen is a communal one shared across up to five other rooms. A couple who cook most of their vegan + gluten-free food need dedicated, cookable counter space they control; a strangers' shared student kitchen (GF cross-contamination, competing for the stove, no reservable prep space) is the opposite of that. The room itself is clean, bright and furnished, and one room even has a large desk with an office chair (good for one remote worker), and the location near Herzliya station is excellent for Tel Aviv — but the shared-flat format also collides head-on with their need for quiet. Not a fit.
+
  • Central-east Herzliya, ~900m (12-min walk) from Herzliya railway station — 11-min train to Tel Aviv Center every ~15 min, plus frequent buses (501/502/47/48/247); no car needed
  • Rooms are clean, bright, freshly furnished (double beds, wardrobes, AC in every room); overall condition is good
  • One room has a large desk + office chair — a real workspace for one person
  • Price ₪4,157/mo is under the ₪4,500 budget (but this is per-room in a share, not a whole apartment)
  • Airbnb window shows all 61 days available with short min-nights, so a ~2-month Sept–Nov stay fits the calendar
–
  • Kitchen — the top criterion — is not shown in any photo and is communal, shared across a 6-room flat; impossible to verify oven, stovetop, or any usable prep space, and a shared student kitchen is unworkable for cooking most of your meals
  • Gluten-free cooking in a strangers' shared kitchen means constant cross-contamination risk with no dedicated surfaces
  • It's a private room in a shared apartment for students/young professionals — not a self-contained home for a couple
  • A 6-room share is inherently noisy and social — directly conflicts with their value of quiet
  • Two remote workers would be sharing one room and one desk, plus competing with roommates for common space
?
  • Availability: listed as available for the full 61-day window with short minimum nights, so short-term is fine — but confirm a couple (2 people) can book one room
  • If ever tempted, you'd need to see actual photos of the shared kitchen and learn how many roommates share it before this could even be evaluated
  • The whole premise (private room in a student share) is a mismatch for a self-catering couple who value quiet — better to look at self-contained one-bedroom units
7
One-person studio, microwave-only kitchenette — skip SKIP
Tel Aviv · ₪4,140/mo rent · airbnb · added 2026-07-14
≥₪4,790/mo all-inrent only · over ₪4,500 all-in
rent ₪4,140 + arnona n/a + est. utilities ₪650 · missing arnona not stated
The kitchen is a token kitchenette and fails the #1 criterion outright: a tiny square sink with only a ~40cm ledge beside it, a mini-fridge underneath, a microwave and an electric kettle — no stovetop and no oven in any of the 7 photos, and no counter/prep space to even add a portable one. On top of that the listing is out of scope: the description explicitly says "Suitable for ONE guest only - a folding couch opens to a single bed," so it cannot house a couple for two months. There is one wall-mounted fold-down table with a single folding chair — no way for two people to work remotely. It is clean, central (near the Carmel Market), ground-floor and within budget, but none of that rescues it.
+
  • Central Tel Aviv location (near Carmel Market/Nachalat Binyamin) — walkable to the shuk for fresh vegan & gluten-free produce and to frequent buses
  • Within budget at ~₪3,942/mo
  • Clean, bright, well-organized; ground-floor with its own entrance
  • Short stays accepted (min 3 nights) and fully available across the window
–
  • Explicitly "suitable for ONE guest only" — single folding bed; cannot house a couple for two months (out of scope)
  • Token kitchenette: NO stovetop, NO oven — only a microwave and kettle — and essentially no counter/prep space, so cooking most of their own food is impossible; no room to add a portable hob either
  • No workspace for two: a single wall fold-down table with one folding chair
  • Kitchen sink/prep ledge sits right against the tiny wet-room bathroom
  • Central Carmel Market area is busy and touristy — not quiet
?
  • This is a one-person unit — only worth a message if the host has a larger apartment; otherwise skip
  • Short-term availability is fine (min 3 nights, 61/61 days open), so that is not the blocker
0
